* Please submit your application in English; it’s our company language so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join 💕
* We treat all candidates equally: If you are interested please apply through our application system - any correspondence should come from there! Our lovely support team isn't able to pass on any calls/ emails our way - and this makes sure that the candidate experience is smooth and fair to everyone 😊
* We can only achieve our goals if our team reflects the diversity of the world around us - and that starts with you, hitting apply, even if you are worried you might not tick all the boxes. We embrace and encourage people from all backgrounds to apply - regardless of race/ethnicity, colour, religion, nationality, gender, sex, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, neurodiversity, socio-economic status, culture or beliefs
* When you submit an application we process your personal data as a data processor. Find out more about how your data is used in the FAQs section at the bottom of our jobs page

title: Commercial Data Specialist (Full-Time)
description:
TL;DR
   

  

 We’re hiring a Commercial Data Specialist to own Tendium’s commercial data foundation end-to-end, from integrations and data pipelines to analytics and insights. You’ll make sure CRM, product, and financial data flows cleanly across our tech stack, power decision-making for Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success, and together with our tech team, you will build scalable models using tools like SQL, Python, dbt, and Airflow. If you like working close to the business and turning messy data into trusted insights, this role is for you.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We’re looking for a technically strong, analytically driven Commercial Data Specialist to own and develop the data foundation of Tendium’s commercial organisation. You’ll join our Commercial Operations team, a cross-functional team responsible for shaping how Tendium’s commercial organisation operates, scales, and collaborates across Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, Finance, and Product.
   

  

 In this role, you’ll work hands-on with CRM data, financial data, product usage data, and internal analytics initiatives, with clear ownership of how data flows and integrates across Tendium’s commercial tech stack. You’ll ensure that data is not only insightful, but also well-orchestrated, reliable, and scalable across systems.
   

  

 You’ll build the analytical and integration layer that supports forecasting, performance tracking, segmentation, and market expansion, helping teams understand what’s happening, why it’s happening, and what to do next.
   

  

 The Commercial Operations team sits at the centre of Tendium’s revenue organisation, defining ways of working, owning the systems that power commercial execution, and enabling tighter collaboration between revenue teams. Within this context, your focus will be on data, analytics, and technical foundations, making sure our numbers are trusted, comparable, actively used, and seamlessly connected across tools and markets.
   

  

 If you enjoy working close to the business, collaborating across functions, challenging assumptions, and building data structures and integrations that scale, this role is for you.
   

  

 Why Tendium?
   

  

 Tendium is the global pioneer in AI-driven public tender management, transforming how companies discover, analyse, and win government contracts. Our platform turns complex processes into something simple and transparent, empowering businesses to compete fairly for billions in public sector opportunities.We’ve been building our AI-native platform since 2018, leveraging a vast data moat, agentic AI, and tens of thousands of hours of R&D. Today, we serve large numbers of customers across Europe and are rapidly scaling.
   

  

 If you’re ready to make an impact and grow with us, we want to hear from you.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

 You’ll own and develop the data foundation of Tendium’s commercial organisation, ensuring that marketing, sales, and customer success decisions are grounded in reliable, well-structured, and actionable data.
   

  

* Own and develop the data foundation of Tendium’s commercial organisation, ensuring that marketing, sales, and customer success decisions are grounded in reliable, well-structured, and actionable data.
* Take specific responsibility for integrations between applications in Tendium’s commercial and data tech stack, ensuring consistent data flow across CRM, product analytics, finance systems, and internal tools.
* Own and develop the analysis and application layer across Tendium’s core commercial data domains, including financial data, CRM, product usage data, and internal data projects, ensuring robust data models, clear definitions, and reliable inputs for reporting, forecasting, and decision-making.
* Together with our tech organisation, you will design, and maintain data transformation and orchestration pipelines using tools such as dbt, Airflow, or equivalent, ensuring scalable, maintainable, and well-documented data workflows.
* Own CRM and revenue data quality at scale by implementing validation rules, enrichment logic, deduplication routines, and monitoring frameworks to ensure high data integrity over time.
* Create and maintain dashboards, reports, and analytical views that give commercial teams clear insight into funnel performance, pipeline health, revenue drivers, and cohort behaviour.
* Run funnel and performance analysis, including stage-level conversion analysis, time-to-close diagnostics, churn and expansion insights, and market-level comparisons.
* Build a data-driven Customer Success engine by developing analytics for customer health, churn, renewals, onboarding, and product adoption; surfacing upsell and cross-sell opportunities from usage and customer data; and enabling proactive account management, capacity planning, and insight-driven decisions across HubSpot and product analytics tools.
* Support international expansion by scaling data structures, integration logic, segmentation models, attribution frameworks, and reporting across markets.
* Explore and apply AI and advanced analytics to forecasting, lead scoring, segmentation, and operational decision-making.


 Who You Are
   

  

 You’re early in your career but you already know this: you thrive in order, structure, and precision. You love helping teams work smarter.
   

  

**You Have**
* At least 3–5 years of experience in revenue analytics, RevOps, business analytics, data analytics, or a similar data-heavy commercial role
* Solid knowledge of SQL and experience querying and modelling data across multiple sources
* Working experience with Python (or similar languages) for data analysis, automation, or data transformation
* Experience working with data transformation and orchestration tools such as dbt, Airflow, or equivalent
* A strong understanding of B2B revenue metrics and funnels (pipeline, ARR, churn, expansion, cohort analysis, attribution, conversion rates)
* Experience building dashboards, reports, and analytical models that support both operational and strategic decision-making
* Comfort working with imperfect data and improving it through structure, validation, integrations, and clear definitions
* The ability to translate business questions into analytical approaches, data models, and clear recommendations
* High attention to detail, strong ownership mindset, and a preference for data-backed decisions over assumptions
* Fluency in English (Swedish is a plus)
* A degree in Industrial Economics and Management, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, Business, or a related field


 How We Hire
   

  

* Submit your application
* Complete a short introduction call with our team.
* Take a set of assessment tests to help us understand who you are.
* Join us for an interview where we dive deeper into your experiences.
* Complete a take-home case exercise
* Meet the wider team in a final conversation before we decide on next steps.


 Application
   

  

 We look forward to your application. We recruit on an ongoing basis, so apply as soon as possible. Successful candidates will initially be invited to an initial interview where you will have the opportunity to meet the team.
   

  

 To make this process even more efficient, feel free to sign in to your Connect profile and add your references at any stage. If you want to know more about our culture and what you can expect when working at Tendium you can read more about it
 *here.*
**Equality and inclusion statement**
 At Tendium, we don’t just value diversity, we celebrate it. We’re building a workplace where everyone should feel that they belong, where your voice is heard, and your experiences are respected. Different perspectives fuel our creativity and drive innovation, and we’re always looking for people who can bring something new to the team. Whoever you are, wherever you're from, we’d love to hear from you. Let’s shape the future together.
   

  

 At Tendium we’re using data to transform public procurement, giving our clients better access to public tenders and helping governments to curb waste and enhance transparency.
   

  

 For more information contact career@tendium.com

title: Senior Application Expert - Anaplan
description:
**Job Description**

  

  





 As a Senior Application Expert, you will work in a team that delivers high-quality Anaplan solutions for financial forecasting. You'll need a strong IT and tech engineering background, expertise in model building, data integration, and familiarity with finance/controlling.
   

  





  

  





**WHAT YOU'LL DO:**



* Develop Anaplan technical solutions for business needs, including data models, in a digestible way.
* Build the models in Anaplan to support the planning process of the specific business unit.
* Ensure every build meets the highest standards always with quality assured data.
* Continually work on security, maintainability, and supportability of the developed solutions.
* Collaborate with a wider team of Product Manager, Solution Architects, Business & Application experts as a part of an agile team.
* Work closely with the source and/or target technical solutions for data integrations.



  

  





**WHO YOU ARE:**



* 2+ years of experience building financial forecasting solutions (preferably in Anaplan).
* Strong technical background in building models and designing data integrations, as well as having the ability to transform business needs into a working technical solution.
* Experience of testing, troubleshooting, and securing technical solutions
* Experience in the finance/controlling area and familiar with terms as Cost Centers and Profit & Loss reporting.
* Experience of building strategies for integrating data into the models.
* Experience of working in an agile way, with the confidence to continuously look for and suggest improvements.
* Experience/interest in AI and how to use it to automate work and increase efficiency
* Show pride in solid documentation as well as building well-designed solutions.
* Be self-driven and well organized, with the ability to communicate in a clear and supportive way.


**Additional Information**

***Please note***
 : As our culture and teamwork are very important to us, four days (80%) per week of in-office presence in our office are required.
   

  





 This is a full-time position with placement in
 **Stockholm** 
 .
   

  





  

  





**WHO WE ARE:**
  

 Shaping the future of fashion with people, data, and tech. The fashion and retail industries are going through a transformation, driven by customers' technology and sustainability expectations. At H&M Group, we want to shape the future of fashion and lifestyle by harnessing the power of smart tech and data. With our 74-year history of innovation, we understand the need to collaborate and co-create with engineers and tech specialists around the world to achieve our vision.
   

  





  

  





**WHAT WE OFFER!** 
  

  

 You are joining a unique value-driven culture, a large tech network and community where you can be yourself. Besides the obvious perks such as staff discount card, flexible work life, learning communities, wellness benefits, parental benefits etc. There are endless opportunities to experiment and grow in any direction that you want, and when you grow, we grow. Being a major player gives us countless opportunities to make a real impact and shape the future.

title: (Senior) IT Consultant - Enterprise Solutions | BCG Platinion
description:
Who We Are
   

  

 Do you want to turn the challenges of digitalization into the solutions of the future? Join BCG Platinion in tackling the core IT issues of leading global companies. Deploy customized platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizations to leverage the digital potential of our customers and drive true innovation, with state-of-the-art tools such as cloud computing, AI, blockchain, microservices, and containerization. Add your individual strengths to our unique team, always there to support you. Together with Boston Consulting Group and our clients, we turn digital visions into reality.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 For our Nordic offices (Stockholm, Oslo, Helsinki and Copenhagen), we are looking for (Senior) IT Consultants for our Enterprise Solutions chapter! About The Job At BCG Platinion, we provide sound advice on all relevant IT issues. In particular, the migration to the SAP S/4HANA solution against the background of the planned mainstream maintenance presents our customers with special challenges. The typical questions our customers have range from justification and business benefits of the SAP S/4 HANA Transition to optimized roadmaps taking into account the implications on overall architecture and also alternative solutions and platforms. To serve our customers even better, we would like to strengthen our Enterprise Solutions team. You work together with colleagues from Platinion and/or the Boston Consulting Group on local and international customer projects. Together with our customers, you actively develop superior, business value driven ERP and application architecture strategies, concepts and roadmaps and apply your sound technical and process-related understanding. Last but not least, you enjoy your work and embody the 'BCG Platinion Spirit' with your hands-on manner.
   

  

 What You'll Bring
   

  

**This Is How You Make a Difference**
* An above-average degree in computer science, business informatics, business administration or a related field
* At least 3 years of relevant experience in the context of strategic ERP/CRM/SCM projects
* Technical knowledge along typical end-to-end processes relevant in the enterprise solutions context (Purchase-2-Pay, Order-2-Cash, Lead-2-Quote, Record-2-Report, Hire-2-Retire etc.)
* General knowledge of wider portfolio of industry specific solutions, solution add-on’s and analytical tools Exceptional analytical and conceptual skills
* Fluent in one Nordic Language and English (written and spoken)
* Strong client and result orientation
* Team spirit, communication and presentation skills as well as intellectual agility
* Ability to align individuals and organizations to a common goal


 Who You'll Work With
   

  

**Shape The World Of Tomorrow**
* Supporting the client in ERP/CRM/SCM projects along the entire supply chain: from requirements analysis to solution design/technical concept, implementation, testing, training and rollout
* Design / definition of specific ERP/CRM/SCM solutions along the value chain of our clients and the required functional modules Introduction of 'best practices' in the evaluation, specification and implementation of user requirements, in particular with a view to reducing one-off and maintenance costs
* Technical management of implementation and migration projects or in specific domains Requirements management within the scope of enterprise solutions projects, incl. the management of specialist concept workshops with business process managers
* Control and prototyping of customizing of ERP/CRM/SCM solutions Support in the sale of large Enterprise Solutions projects by providing technical and methodological expertise
* Further development of the content of the enterprise solutions topics at BCG Platinion, e.g., through participation in conferences, in the creation of a training catalog or with technical articles


 Additional info
   

  

**How You Can Grow With Us**
 We guarantee constant development incentives in an open and rewarding work environment! Together with a dedicated team of talented colleagues, you get to work on new challenges every day. Individual training offers and a culture of open feedback will provide the framework needed to explore and perfect personal strengths. Our modern structures allow focusing on the essence of our work - with easy travel booking, an excellent back-office support and comprehensive Knowledge Center. With our regular office Fridays and legendary events, we nurture a strong and close team network beyond our case work.
   

  

**Become a Part Of Our Team**
 Apply over our online portal now, with your CV, a relevant project list, and all pertinent references.
   

  

*BCG Platinion is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to national origin, color, age, sex, gender identity / expression, sexual orientation, religion, disability or any other protected characteristic.*
*#BCGPlatinion*
 Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
   

  

 BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

title: Software Engineer within eHealth
description:
*Cambio is one of the Nordics' leading suppliers in eHealth with a comprehensive offering for the entire health and care chain. Our eHealth solutions support health care professionals in their daily work and offer patients safer and more easily accessible care. We are proud to be an important part of Swedish healthcare. We are growing continuously and now have about 1000 employees in several countries.*
**The position**
 As a Software Engineer with a backend focus, you will be part of a highly skilled, ambitious tech team building
 **an AI-driven system that operates at the core of clinical workflows** 
 . Cambio Ambient Scribe is an ambient AI assistant that listens to and documents patient–clinician conversations in real time. By reducing administrative burden and saving up to two hours per day for healthcare professionals, the product helps clinicians focus on what truly matters: the patient.
 *Read more* 
 here.
   

  

 In this role, you will take
 **clear ownership of backend design, implementation, and scalability** 
 , ensuring that the system is secure, performant, and ready to scale from a thousand to tens of thousands of users. You will work closely with other engineers with different focus areas, such as frontend/UX, AI, and full-stack.
   

  

 This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys writing production-quality code, designing robust systems, and taking responsibility for technical decisions in a complex and meaningful domain.
   

  

**Some of the things you’ll do**
* Design, build, and evolve backend services for a mission-critical healthcare AI product.
* Develop and maintain scalable APIs and services, primarily using Python and modern backend frameworks.
* Design data models and storage solutions that ensure performance, reliability, and long-term scalability.
* Build systems with security, privacy, and compliance as first-class concerns.
* Work with container-based architectures and contribute to CI/CD, observability, and operational excellence.
* Collaborate closely with AI, DevOps, and product teams to deliver a high-quality, end-to-end solution.
* Take ownership of technical decisions and help shape the backend architecture as the product and platform evolve.


**We offer**
 At Cambio, you don’t just build software – you build technology that
 **directly improves healthcare** 
 .
   

  

**You’ll Get The Opportunity To**
* Work on a cutting-edge AI product that is deeply integrated into COSMIC, Sweden’s most widely used electronic health record system.
* Be part of a small, highly competent team with strong ownership, short decision paths, and a startup mindset within a stable company.
* Influence architecture and technical direction in a rapidly scaling product.
* Grow with the role – both technically and in responsibility – while working on problems that truly matter to society.


**About You**
 We believe you are a Senior Software Engineer with a backend focus who is comfortable taking responsibility for complex systems and enjoys solving hard problems. You have a calm confidence in your technical skills, but you’re also curious, humble, and continuously learning.
   

  

 Rather than checking every single box, what matters most is that you have a
 **strong foundation and the ability to design and scale backend systems in production** 
 . You enjoy writing code, care deeply about quality, and understand the importance of security and reliability in healthcare systems.
   

  

**We see that you tick some of the following boxes:**
* Strong backend development experience, with Python as a core language.
* Around 5+ years of professional experience in backend engineering or similar roles.
* Experience designing and scaling backend systems in production environments.
* You are comfortable using modern AI tools as a natural part of your development workflow.
* Solid understanding of security principles in backend and cloud-based systems.
* Experience working with containers (Docker) and modern deployment pipelines (CI/CD).
* Experience with relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis) and data modeling for scale.
* Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, or Azure.
* Ability to take ownership, communicate technical decisions, and work collaboratively in a team.


**Bonus Qualifications**
* Experience with microservice architectures.
* Experience with observability, monitoring, and performance optimization.
* Previous work in healthcare, healthtech, or other regulated environments.


**Place of employment** 
 : Umeå or Stockholm
   

  

**Scope** 
 : Full-time, hybrid work model (three days per week in-office)
   

  

**Form of employment:** 
 Permanent employment, 6 months trial period
   

  

**Additional Information:** 
 The role involves access to sensitive information, therefore a background check will be conducted on the final candidate.
   

  

 We review applications and conduct interviews on a rolling basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible via the link.
   

  

**We look forward to receiving your application!**
**CAMBIO- FOR A HEALTHIER TOMORROW**
 Welcome to a company where healthcare is improved every day. Here you will work with leading technology in a collaborative, trusting and empowering environment where your contribution will be appreciated and celebrated. In our caring culture you will have the opportunity to pursue different paths of professional growth. Seize the opportunity to do the most important work of your life
 **.** 
 Read more about us
 **here!**
**A healthier tomorrow also means taking care of those who make it possible -** 
 explore our employee benefits
   

  

 Learn more about our recruitment process here
   

  

 Are you interested in Cambio but you don’t feel that this position is the perfect match? Check out our other open positions at our career site.
   

  

 Cambio Healthcare Systems is Sweden’s leading suppliers of healthcare information systems and a growing player in the European market with just over 100,000 users across general and university hospitals, specialist units and outpatient units. Through the use of our integrated solutions, our customers provide services to 4 million patients.

title: Chefsarkitekt IT
description:
Om Tjänsten
   

  

**Ta ledningen i Riksbyggens digitaliseringsresa – bli vår nästa IT-arkitekt!**
 Har du tidigare erfarenhet av arbete med IT arkitektur, data analytics, leda agilt utvecklingsarbete och coacha utvecklare? Lockas du av möjligheten att ta större ansvar och en chans att verkligen påverka Riksbyggens digitaliseringsresa?
   

  

 Ansök idag!
   

  

**Vad ska du göra?**
 Riksbyggen är ett kooperativt företag som utvecklar attraktiva boendemiljöer. Med 60 kontor över hela landet och ca 2200 anställda är Riksbyggen en av Sveriges största fastighetsförvaltare med bostadsrättsföreningar, företag och offentliga fastighetsägare som kunder. I över 80 år har Riksbyggen byggt och förvaltat fastigheter över hela Sverige. Trygga, härliga hem med plats för äventyr, fest och drömmar. För allt det som gör livet större och roligare.
   

  

 Nu letar Riksbyggen efter en Chefsarkitekt IT. Det är en ny roll som kommer att rapportera till CDO&CIO för Riksbyggen och du kommer att vara del av en IT-organisation bestående av ett 40-tal medarbetare och ca 20 konsulter. I den här rollen kommer du ansvara för Riksbyggens IT arkitektur och Data analytics.
   

  

 I rollen erbjuds du stor frihet under ansvar där du har en central roll som ingår i bolagets IT-ledningsgrupp. Du får möjlighet att verkligen påverka och ingå i ett bolag som ligger i framkant inom fastighetsbranschen när det kommer till digitalisering.
   

  

**Hos oss gör du skillnad**
 Du ingår i ledningsgruppen för IT & Digital förvaltning och utveckling och rapporterar till Riksbyggens CDO&CIO. Som Chefsarkitekt IT leder du planering, utveckling och uppföljning av Riksbyggens IT arkitektur och Data analytics. Tjänsten innefattar personalansvar och konsultansvar för de som arbetar med arkitektur och data analytics. Gruppen består idag av 7 personer (varav 2 konsulter). Riksbyggen har en hybrid IT miljö med system i egen driftmiljö, Microsoft Azure and SaaS tjänster.
   

  

**Arbetsuppgifter**
 Du kommer bland annat ansvara för:
   

  

* Tekniska vägval och utveckling av IT arkitektur och Data analytics förmågan i Riksbyggen
* Säkerställa god kommunikation rörande arkitektur och teknikval med interna intressenter som processansvariga, produktägare, projektledare och IT ledning
* Leda medarbetare och konsulter
* Rekrytering inom ansvarsområdet för att långsiktigt minska antalet konsulter
* Följa upp kostnader och budget
* Stödja CDO&CIO och IT-ledning i IT strategiska frågor och samverka med övriga it chefer i det dagliga operativa arbetet


**Du bidrar med din kompetens och ditt engagemang**
 Som person är du engagerad och förtroendeingivande med en välutvecklad samarbetsförmåga. Du är också trygg i din kompetens och har en stark vilja att bidra till organisationens framgång. Vidare så har du har en god kommunikativ såväl som teknisk förmåga och är en bra ledare. Du är strukturerad, prestigelös, drivande och tar tag i de uppgifter som behöver lösas med ett positivt och fokuserat engagemang. Du skapar förtroende och är bekväm att arbeta både på strategisk såväl som på operativ nivå.
   

  

 Vi ser också att du har:
   

  

* Universitetsutbildning som ingenjör, ekonom, systemvetare eller likvärdigt
* Systematiskt tanke- och arbetssätt: Förmågan att prioritera och genomföra aktiviteter inom ekonomiska ramar och tidplaner
* God kunskap om agila utvecklingsmetoder (gärna Scaled Agile Framework, SAFe)
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av IT utveckling i seniora tekniska roller (senior utvecklare, IT arkitekt, techlead eller motsvarande)
* Erfarenhet av ledarskap och personalansvar
* God teknisk kunskap av flera av de verktyg och programmeringsspråk Riksbyggen använder
* Erfarenhet av IT ledningsprocesser, arkitektarbete och projektledning


 De främsta tekniska kunskaperna som efterfrågas är:
   

  

* DevOps (CI/CD)
* Microsoft Azure (Functions, App services, Graph API, Service bus)
* Power platform
* Microsoft Fabric och data governance
* Integrationsplattformar och API-baserad arkitektur


 Meriterande med goda kunskaper inom Dynamics365 och AI utveckling
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 Du kommer att ha placering på Riksbyggens huvudkontor på Kungsbron i centrala Stockholm. Flexibelt startdatum, med hänsyn till din uppsägningstid. Riksbyggen tillämpar provanställning.
   

  

 Riksbyggen strävar efter en jämn könsfördelning och ser mångfald som en styrka.
   

  

**Ansökan**
 Din ansökan skickar du till oss via Riksbyggen.se – Lediga jobb. Sista ansökningsdag är 13 mars 2026. Vi gör löpande urval så sök redan idag! Vi ser fram emot din ansökan.
   

  

 Vid frågor kontakta Per Karmteg epost: per.karmteg@riksbyggen.se
   

  

**Övrigt** 
 Som anställd på Riksbyggen förväntas du också vara ledamot i någon/några bostadsrättsföreningar, i de fall du har ledamotsuppdrag så utgår ett ansvarsarvode. Klicka HÄR om du är nyfiken på Riksbyggen. Till rekryteringskonsulter, annonssäljare etc. Vi har redan beslutat vilka vi samarbetar med vid rekryteringar samt var vi vill synas. Vi undanber oss därför kontakt från försäljare i dessa frågor.
   

  

  

  

 Visa fler lediga jobb
   

  

 arrow\_forward

title: Implementation Specialist - Norway
description:
**About Intric**
 Intric is accelerating its growth, backed by new funding and strong demand from both existing and new customers.
   

  

 Our platform, Intric.ai, is transforming how work gets done – particularly within the public sector and among organizations that prioritize data sovereignty. We make advanced AI accessible, secure, and practical. Today, we hold a market-leading position within our niche, and we are continuing to scale across Europe.
   

  

 We are now looking for an Implementation Specialist who wants to be part of building something meaningful from the ground up.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As an Implementation Specialist, you play a critical role in ensuring our customers succeed with Intric. You are the bridge between our platform and the organizations that rely on it.
   

  

 Your focus is not only on understanding customer needs, but on translating those needs into real, value-creating implementations. You educate, guide, and inspire users to adopt AI in ways that meaningfully improve their daily work.
   

  

 This is a hands-on role where you work closely with customers, internal teams, and subject-matter experts to ensure smooth onboarding, strong engagement, and long-term success.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

* Lead customer onboarding and implementation projects from start to finish
* Educate and inspire users on how to leverage the platform effectively
* Analyze customer workflows and identify opportunities where AI can create efficiency and impact
* Facilitate seamless integration of Intric into day-to-day operations
* Collaborate with customer stakeholders and internal product teams to ensure successful outcomes
* Drive engagement and long-term adoption by building strong, trust-based relationships


 Who You Are
   

  

 You are someone who enjoys working closely with people while understanding systems and processes at a deeper level.
   

  

 You:
   

  

* Communicate complex ideas clearly and persuasively
* Have a service-oriented and pedagogical mindset
* Learn new technology quickly and feel comfortable navigating digital tools
* Are curious about how organizations operate and enjoy understanding workflows
* Thrive in collaborative environments and work well with experts across different domains
* Take ownership and proactively drive projects forward


**Must-Haves**
* Strong communication skills in English and Norwegian both written and verbal
* Experience working with customers in a consultative or implementation-focused role
* Ability to understand and map business processes
* High level of structure, ownership, and follow-through
* Genuine interest in AI and how technology can improve real-world operations


**Why Join Intric**
* Rapid career development in a fast-moving sector
* Competitive compensation package including salary and stock options
* Ownership and influence: bring your ideas to life
* Be part of a visionary team shaping the future of AI in Europe


**Practical Information**
 Location: Primarily remote with occasional travel to the local office or regional hubs
   

  

 Travel: Regular travel within Norway and occasional cross-market collaboration
   

  

**Benefits**
* Competitive salary and stock options
* 25 days of paid vacation
* Wellness allowance
* Rapid career development in a fast-growing AI company


 Culture at Intric
   

  

 At Intric, we are building secure AI solutions for organizations that truly matter. We work with governments and enterprises where quality, responsibility, and trust are non-negotiable.
   

  

 We value ownership, high standards, and thoughtful execution. Titles matter less than contribution. If you see something that can be improved, you take initiative. If something is unclear, you ask questions. If a customer faces a challenge, you lean in.
   

  

 We combine ambition with collaboration. We move fast, but we care deeply about doing things right. As we continue scaling across Europe, every team member plays a meaningful role in shaping both the company and the culture.

title: System Architect
description:
**System Architect - Business IT** 




 📍Mariehamn eller Stockholm
 



  





**Vill du vara med och forma framtidens finansiella IT-lösningar i Norden?** 




 Crosskey är ett nordiskt fintechbolag i framkant som utvecklar säkra, skalbara och affärskritiska lösningar för bank- och finanssektorn. Nu söker vi en System Architect till vårt team Business IT - en nyckelroll där du får kombinera strategi, teknik och affärsnytta.
 



  





**Om företaget** 




 Med rötter i Ålandsbanken har vi byggt en djup förståelse för finansvärlden. Vår resa mot självständighet växte fram ur en vilja att utmana det etablerade och betrakta branschen ur ett nytt, innovativt perspektiv. I dag är Crosskey en sammansmältning av dessa två världar - expertis kombinerad med nytänkande, gedigen erfarenhet förenad med snabbhet och flexibilitet.
 



  





 Vårt huvudkontor i Mariehamn på Åland speglar denna filosofi på ett naturligt sätt. Här befinner vi oss mitt i den skandinaviska teknikutvecklingen, samtidigt som vi står stadigt i våra egna värderingar och perspektiv. I en tid där finanssektorn präglas av ökande komplexitet, omfattande regelverk och snabb teknologisk utveckling, gör Crosskeys unika synsätt oss till en stark och pålitlig partner för att navigera i ett ständigt föränderligt landskap.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som System Architect i teamet Business IT blir du en central del av vårt arkitekt- och driftteam bestående av cirka 9 administratörer och arkitekter. Du ansvarar för att designa, vidareutveckla och säkra vår Microsoftbaserade IT-miljö - både för interna behov och i nära samarbete med våra kunder. Du arbetar med hela livscykeln: från arkitektur och design till implementation, migrering och vidareutveckling av moderna lösningar inom Windows Server, Azure, Microsoft 365 och Identity Management.
 



  





**Dina ansvarsområden** 



* Designa, dokumentera och kvalitetssäkra lösningsarkitektur för komplexa IT-miljöer
* Leda tekniska diskussioner och fungera som rådgivare för kunder och interna team
* Säkerställa att lösningar följer best practices inom säkerhet, compliance och prestanda
* Delta i och driva migreringsprojekt till molnplattformar (Azure, Microsoft 365, SharePoint)
* Utvärdera nya teknologier och bidra till Crosskeys tekniska roadmap



  





**Vi tror att du har** 



* Djup teknisk kompetens inom flera av följande områden:
* Windows Server & Active Directory
* Microsoft Azure (IaaS, PaaS, säkerhet, nätverk)
* Microsoft 365 (Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint)
* Identity Management (Entra ID, MFA, Conditional Access)
* Endpoint Management (PC, Mac, mobila enheter)
* Virtualiseringsplattformar (VMware, Hyper-V, OpenShift, Nutanix AHV, KVM)
* Erfarenhet av arkitekturdesign, dokumentation och lösningsförslag
* Förmåga att kommunicera tekniska koncept till både tekniska och icke-tekniska intressenter
* Meriterande:
* Erfarenhet av Infrastructure as Code (t.ex. Terraform)
* Microsoft-certifieringar, exempelvis
 *Azure Solutions Architect Expert*
* Erfarenhet av API-integrationer



  





**Därför ska du välja Crosskey** 




 Hos Crosskey blir du en del av ett nytänkande nordiskt fintechbolag där människor, innovation och kvalitet står i centrum. Vi erbjuder en flexibel och inkluderande arbetskultur som präglas av frihet under ansvar, öppen dialog och kontinuerlig feedback. Här får du arbeta med spännande och samhällskritiska lösningar inom bank och finans, tillsammans med engagerade och kunniga kollegor som gärna delar med sig av sin kompetens. Du får möjlighet att påverka både teknikval och arbetssätt, utvecklas i din roll och vara med och forma framtidens finansiella IT-lösningar. Vi älskar utmaningar och drivs av att göra våra kunder ännu mer framgångsrika.
 



  





**Placeringsort:** 
 Mariehamn eller Stockholm - det tillkommer resor till huvudkontoret i Mariehamn vid tillfälle
 



**Anställningsform:** 
 Tillsvidare, heltid
 



  





**Låter det intressant?** 




 Välkommen att bli en del av Crosskey och forma framtidens finansiella IT-lösningar tillsammans med oss. Då vi förhåller oss till GDPR tar vi ej emot ansökningar via epost. Välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Test Engineer
description:
Nu söker vi för kunds räkning en Test Engineer till ett högteknologiskt bolag som utvecklar avancerade systemlösningar för krävande miljöer. Produkterna kombinerar mjukvara, hårdvara, sensorteknik, där kvalitet och stabilitet är helt avgörande. Det här är en roll för dig som vill arbeta praktiskt med mjukvarutest i en systemnära miljö, där du får arbeta nära både hårdvara och utveckling.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Du blir en del av ett tekniskt team med bred kompetens inom system, elektronik och integration. I den här rollen ligger tyngdpunkten på mjukvarutest och verifiering, men alltid i ett större systemperspektiv.
 



  





 Arbetet innebär att sätta upp och konfigurera miljöer, genomföra tester, analysera resultat och felsöka när något inte fungerar som det ska. Du jobbar nära utvecklare och är med och säkerställer att systemen fungerar som helhet, inte bara i teorin, utan i praktiken. Rollen kan anpassas beroende på din erfarenhet. Har du mer erfarenhet kan du ta större ansvar för teststruktur och metodik. Är du mer junior kommer du initialt arbeta mer med genomförande och analys.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter** 



* Konfigurera och anpassa testmiljöer
* Genomföra system- och mjukvarutester
* Felsöka i samverkan mellan mjukvara, operativsystem och hårdvara
* Dokumentera testresultat och avvikelser
* Bidra till utveckling av testmetoder och struktur
* Ta fram enklare script eller verktyg för test och konfiguration



  





**Vem vi söker** 




 Vi söker dig som har ett starkt teknikintresse och trivs i en praktisk, systemnära miljö.
 



 Du kan komma från ett mjukvaruhåll, kanske med erfarenhet av test, verifiering eller programmering och vara nyfiken på att arbeta närmare hårdvaran. Alternativt har du redan erfarenhet av systemtest och vill fortsätta utvecklas i en tekniskt komplex miljö.
 



  





**Vi tror att du:** 



* Har eftergymnasial utbildning inom IT, systemutveckling, elektronik eller liknande
* Har erfarenhet av mjukvarutest
* Är självgående och trygg i att driva ditt arbete framåt
* Har en god förståelse för hur olika delar i ett system hänger ihop



  





 Meriterande:
 


* Erfarenhet av Windows-miljöer
* Grundläggande scripting (PowerShell, Python eller liknande)
* Erfarenhet av test i system där mjukvara och hårdvara samverkar



  





**Övrigt:** 



* Placering: Lidingö (office first, med viss flexibilitet vid behov)
* Arbetstid: Heltid
* Kundkontakt och arbete på plats hos kund förekommer ca 10% av tiden
* Säkerhetsprövning krävs (lägst nivå 3)
* **Anställningsform:** 
 Det här är ett konsultuppdrag och anställning sker hos A-hub men möjlighet till anställning hos kund efter 12 månader.

title: SAP Procurement Lead
description:
**Job Title: SAP Procurement Lead** 




**Job Location: Stockholm, Sweden** 




**Job Type : Permanent** 




  





***Key Responsibilities:***




  





**• Solution Design & Strategy:** 
 Lead end-to-end SAP procurement implementations, ensuring adherence to Best Practices and Clean Core principles.
 



  





**• System Configuration:** 
 Configure and optimize SAP MM, Ariba (Upstream/Downstream), or Fieldglass modules to meet business requirements.
 



  





**• Stakeholder Management:** 
 Collaborate with business, procurement, and IT teams to translate requirements into technical specifications.
 



  





**• Integration & Support:** 
 Oversee integration with SAP Finance (FI/CO) and third- party tools, providing troubleshooting and high-level support.
 



  





**• Process Improvement:** 
 Drive automation, data accuracy, and procurement transformation projects to maximize value.
 



  





*Skills Required:*




  




* SAP consulting or functional roles, with extensive experience in S/4HANA sourcing and procurement.
* Technical Skills: Deep knowledge of SAP MM, Ariba, and/or SAP Fieldglass.
* Leadership: Experience leading large-scale, global implementation teams.
* Functional Expertise: Strong understanding of purchasing, inventory management and supplier collaboration.

title: Project Manager
description:
We are looking for an experienced Project Manager to lead a major business transformation within the Technical Communication area at a global industrial organization.
   

  

 In this role, you will take full ownership of a transformation initiative focused on processes, tools, and governance within technical documentation. You will coordinate stakeholders across manufacturing, central functions, IT, and technical communication teams in multiple regions.
   

  

 You will structure and lead the project organization, define scope and deliverables, manage budget and timelines, secure governance and reporting, and proactively manage risks and dependencies. A key part of the assignment is driving change, ensuring alignment, engagement, and long term adoption of new ways of working.
   

  

 You will act as the bridge between business and IT, translating operational needs into technical requirements while maintaining a strategic overview of the future documentation landscape.
   

  

**Job Requirements**
**Qualifications**
**We Are Looking For Someone Who Brings**
* Several years of experience leading complex transformation or implementation projects
* Strong understanding of technical documentation environments
* Experience with structured content and XML based authoring
* Knowledge of documentation and translation workflows
* Understanding of metadata structures and governance
* Experience working with SAP equipment structures
* Ability to translate business needs into technical requirements
* Experience coordinating cross functional and international stakeholders
* Experience with systems such as Windchill, Arbortext, SAP, Poka, or SharePoint is considered a strong advantage.


 As a leader, you are self driven, structured, and confident in complex environments. You communicate clearly across organizational levels, remain calm in challenging discussions, and combine strategic thinking with hands on execution.
   

  

**Application procedure**
 A Maandag® recruiter will assess your application as soon as possible to determine whether you are suitable for an open position.
   

  

**About The Company**
 If you are a good match, we would like to receive a CV in WORD format with motivation why you fit this assignment, your availability and any scheduled holidays.
   

  

 Why Maandag Nordic? With us, you’ll get the chance to really put your ideas into action. We offer a work environment where you can grow and learn through hands-on experience - trying things out, making mistakes, and improving.
   

  

 For more than 35 years, Maandag® has been at the forefront of empowering people and organisations. We not only help professionals get jobs, but also connect them with a future that matches their values, desires and ambitions. Because when people are truly in the right place, they can develop themselves, fulfil their dreams and go to work with fresh energy every day. The energy and power released in the process is a catalyst for growth for companies. We started in the Netherlands, but now we also help professionals and organisations in Belgium, Poland, Dubai and Sweden. Our extensive knowledge of different markets, coupled with our wide network, enables us to make perfect matches quickly.

title: Workplace Experience Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.
   

  

**The Role**
 We’re looking for a
 **Workplace Experience Manager** 
 who will keep our Stockholm office running smoothly and help create an inspiring and welcoming environment for everyone at Soundtrack.
   

  

 You will also play an important role in the set-up of our new Hub in New York, including occasional travel and coordination with local vendors and office providers.
   

  

 You’ll be the go-to person for everything from daily operations to IT admin, office logistics and event support - ensuring that things don’t just work, but work well and continuously improve.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Organize and coordinate day-to-day workplace operations, office logistics and internal events
* Act as the primary internal point of contact for workplace-related matters, providing hands-on, structured and solution-oriented support to employees
* Manage office supplies, equipment, facilities coordination and ensure a welcoming, organized and well-functioning office environment
* Own and coordinate device procurement, phone subscriptions, access cards and lifecycle management for employees across locations, including our US team
* Structure and manage onboarding/offboarding processes related to devices, access and workplace setup
* Provide first-line technical support (Google Meet, meeting room technology, basic network troubleshooting, etc.) and coordinate external support when needed
* Set up and host large internal meetings and ensure a seamless hybrid/remote experience
* Identify opportunities to improve processes and continuously enhance the workplace experience
* Drive and coordinate the establishment of our New York Hub, including vendor selection, office setup and travel to New York as needed


**About You**
 You share our belief in the impact of music. You're collaborative and open, and you know how to balance working independently with being a strong team player.
   

  

 You’re the kind of person who naturally takes initiative, keeps things organized, and enjoys helping others. You take ownership, have a hands-on approach and aren’t afraid to solve problems before escalating them.
   

  

 You’re comfortable navigating digital environments and learning new tools. You don’t need to be a technical specialist, but you feel confident taking responsibility for our technical workplace setup.
   

  

 You bring positive energy to the office and enjoy creating an environment where people want to come in and collaborate.
   

  

**Requirements**
* 2+ years of experience in an office operations, workplace, or administrative role with ownership responsibility
* Solid IT knowledge and hands-on experience supporting workplace technology (devices, hybrid meeting solutions, basic network troubleshooting)
* Experience coordinating external service providers and managing vendors
* Strong organizational skills and the ability to prioritize multiple incoming requests
* A proactive, solution-oriented and service-minded mindset
* Fluent in English with strong professional proficiency in Swedish


**Employee Benefits**
* We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
* Generous pension plan
* Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
* Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
* Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
* Daily breakfast served at the office


 At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.
   

  

 We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
   

  

 Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

title: Data & AI Platform - Center of Enablement Manager
description:
Are you excited by the idea of running a modern data platform that our engineers rely on every day? Join us in shaping our architecture, coaching teams, and turning best practice into golden paths that make great engineering the easy choice!
   

  

**Why Sandvik Coromant?**
 We’re a global leader where data, AI, and automation directly power real manufacturing impact – and where your platform becomes the backbone for product teams across the business. We don’t just lead the manufacturing industry – we’re reshaping it. As a global frontrunner in advanced manufacturing, we're on a journey to transform how we work and how we create value.
   

  

 The industry’s evolving fast, and we’re responding with smart, end-to-end solutions powered by bold ideas and passionate people. This is your chance to join a high-trust, empowered team that challenges the status quo and puts customers at the center of everything we do. Let’s shape the future of manufacturing together!
   

  

**Your mission**
 In this position, you lead the strategy, architecture, and operations of our Data and AI Experience Platform as well as our team of Platform Engineers and Architects. You ensure platform reliability, cost compliance, and vendor alignment while driving technical excellence and fostering a strong engineering culture. Acting as our go-to expert for critical challenges, you coach engineers, shape best practices, and enable autonomous product teams through optimized architecture and self-service capabilities. You define standards and services which make it easy for your team to do the right thing while experimenting and building autonomy, compliance, security, cost efficiency and reliability.
   

  

 You lead through example – continuously learning and experimenting with new technologies, building solutions hands-on, and coaching others. Create space and opportunities for team learning, foster collaboration, and confidently make informed technical decisions that inspire trust and growth. Adoption is your success currency, and you constantly find ways in which your team can improve Monthly Active Engineers and Monthly Active Champions on your platform.
   

  

**Location and flexibility**
 The location for this position is either Sandviken or Stockholm, with a hybrid setup where you can combine office and remote work.
   

  

**Your profile**
 We’re looking for someone with several years of experience in engineering and technology roles combined with some experience from leadership and people management. The ideal candidate has a background with at least one of the following roles: Data & Analytics Architect, Platform Architect or AI Architect. Acting in a global environment calls for excellent skills in English, verbally and in writing.
   

  

 You have proven expertise with:
   

  

* Cloud computing service providers, preferably Microsoft Azure.
* Lakehouse service providers, preferably Databricks.
* Embedded Data Warehouse and Analytics providers, preferably SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.
* Expert software engineering skills in Python, SQL, C# or similar languages.


 Success in this role comes from strategic thinking with hands‑on technical curiosity, bringing a natural drive to explore new ideas and solve complex challenges. You lead with openness and empathy, coaching others and building trust across teams while remaining calm and confident in your decisions. With a collaborative mindset and a genuine focus on customer needs, you create clarity, foster autonomy, and inspire a strong engineering culture grounded in learning, inclusivity, and continuous improvement.
   

  

**Our culture**
 Innovation is in our DNA and we’re proud to shape the future of manufacturing in close collaboration with our customers and partners. We believe in an inclusive, equal and open-minded culture, and we nurture our diversities to form a solid foundation for achieving great results. Add fair and rewarding benefits, as well as many different career opportunities in an inspiring global environment. Meet some of your future colleagues at our career page or visit our website, LinkedIn or Facebook page to get to know us further.
   

  

**Contact information**
 Are you curious to know more about this position? Please contact Anton Peters, Head of Data & AI, at anton.peters@sandvik.com
   

  

 We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contact in that matter.
   

  

 Union contacts (Sweden):
   

  

* Thomas Lilja, Unionen, +46 (0)70-261 04 82
* Tord Engström, Akademikerföreningen, +46 (0)76 106 62 87
* Peter Olsson-Andrée, Ledarna, +46 (0)70-222 48 55


 Recruitment Specialist: Therese Rutqvist
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible and no later than February 27, 2026. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e-mail. Job ID: R0087443.
   

  

 As we aim for a fair recruitment process, we utilize assessment tools to safeguard objectivity. When you apply for this job, you will therefore receive an invitation via email to a personality and logic ability test. Feedback comes immediately after the test has been completed and the selection process begins after the application deadline.
   

  

 To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com

title: DevOps Engineer within eHealth
description:
*Cambio is one of the Nordics' leading suppliers in eHealth with a comprehensive offering for the entire health and care chain. Our eHealth solutions support health care professionals in their daily work and offer patients safer and more easily accessible care. We are proud to be an important part of Swedish healthcare. We are growing continuously and now have about 1000 employees in several countries.*
**The position**
 As a DevOps Engineer, you will be part of a highly skilled, ambitious tech team building an AI‑driven system that operates at the core of clinical workflows. Cambio Ambient Scribe is an ambient AI assistant that listens to and documents patient–clinician conversations in real time. By reducing administrative burden and saving up to two hours per day for healthcare professionals, the product helps clinicians focus on what truly matters: the patient.
   

  

*Read more* 
 here.
   

  

 In this role, you will take
 **end‑to‑end ownership of our cloud and platform infrastructure** 
 , ensuring that the system is
 **secure, reliable, and ready to scale** 
 as the product grows from thousands to tens of thousands of users. You will work closely with backend, AI, frontend, and product teams to enable fast, safe, and efficient delivery of an AI‑powered healthcare platform.
   

  

 This is a hands‑on role for someone who enjoys combining
 **infrastructure, automation, and problem‑solving** 
 , and who wants to play a central role in shaping a modern DevOps platform in a regulated and mission‑critical environment.
   

  

**Some of the things you’ll do**
* Own, operate, and evolve our cloud infrastructure, primarily in Azure, with a roadmap toward Kubernetes / OpenShift.
* Build and maintain Kubernetes platforms, including Helm, ingress, RBAC, scheduling, and networking.
* Design and operate secure, automated CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps / GitHub Actions).
* Implement observability and monitoring using tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, or ELK.
* Apply DevSecOps principles across the stack, including identity, secrets management, network policies, and compliance.
* Deploy, monitor, and scale PostgreSQL / Supabase environments, including backup, performance, and reliability.
* Support and optimize GPU‑based AI inference workloads, collaborating closely with backend and AI engineers.
* Act as a technical partner to the development teams, improving deployment, stability, and operational efficiency.


**We offer**
 At Cambio, you don’t just work with infrastructure – you enable technology that
 **directly improves healthcare** 
 .
   

  

**You’ll Get The Opportunity To**
* Work on a cutting‑edge AI product deeply integrated into COSMIC, Sweden’s most widely used electronic health record system.
* Be part of a small, highly competent team with strong ownership, short decision paths, and a startup mindset within a stable company.
* Influence the cloud and platform architecture of a product that is scaling rapidly and handling sensitive healthcare data.
* Grow with the role – both technically and in responsibility – while solving problems that truly matter to society.


**About You**
 We believe you are a
 **Senior DevOps / Cloud Engineer** 
 who is comfortable taking responsibility for complex, production‑critical systems. You combine a strong technical foundation with curiosity, pragmatism, and a drive to continuously improve how systems are built, deployed, and operated.
   

  

 Rather than matching every single technology listed, what matters most is that you are
 **confident designing and running secure, scalable cloud platforms** 
 and enjoy working closely with developers to enable efficient, reliable software delivery.
   

  

**We see that you tick some of the following boxes:**
* Several years of experience (typically 5+) working with DevOps, cloud engineering, or platform engineering in production environments.
* Strong experience with cloud infrastructure, ideally Azure (but experience from AWS or GCP is also valuable).
* Hands‑on experience with containers and Kubernetes, or a clear ambition to move deeper into Kubernetes/OpenShift.
* Experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure automation.
* Solid understanding of security principles across cloud, network, and application layers.
* Experience with monitoring, observability, and operating systems in production.
* Ability to take ownership, communicate technical decisions, and work closely with development teams.
* Comfort using modern AI tools as part of your engineering workflow to improve efficiency and quality.
* Experience with GPU workloads and real‑time AI inference in production.
* Experience with microservices architectures and distributed systems.
* Experience working in regulated environments, such as healthcare or financial services.
* Familiarity with FastAPI / Python applications and how application design impacts infrastructure and operations.


**Place of employment** 
 : Umeå or Stockholm
   

  

**Scope** 
 : Full-time, hybrid work model (three days per week in-office)
   

  

**Form of employment:** 
 Permanent employment, 6 months trial period
   

  

**Additional Information:** 
 The role involves access to sensitive information, therefore a background check will be conducted on the final candidate.
   

  

 We review applications and conduct interviews on a rolling basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible via the link.
   

  

**We look forward to receiving your application!**
**CAMBIO- FOR A HEALTHIER TOMORROW**
 Welcome to a company where healthcare is improved every day. Here you will work with leading technology in a collaborative, trusting and empowering environment where your contribution will be appreciated and celebrated. In our caring culture you will have the opportunity to pursue different paths of professional growth. Seize the opportunity to do the most important work of your life
 **.** 
 Read more about us
 **here!**
**A healthier tomorrow also means taking care of those who make it possible -** 
 explore our employee benefits
   

  

 Learn more about our recruitment process here
   

  

 Are you interested in Cambio but you don’t feel that this position is the perfect match? Check out our other open positions at our career site.
   

  

 Cambio Healthcare Systems is Sweden’s leading suppliers of healthcare information systems and a growing player in the European market with just over 100,000 users across general and university hospitals, specialist units and outpatient units. Through the use of our integrated solutions, our customers provide services to 4 million patients.

title: Head of Demand Gen (AI Startup)
description:
Most marketing hires at this stage want a playbook to follow. We need someone who writes the playbook. You will own a number, control how we market, and have an immense impact. That excites you, not makes you fearful.
 



  





 AutoRFP.ai automates how enterprise sales teams respond to RFPs, security questionnaires, and due diligence requests. Built model-agnostic from day one, deployed across 44 countries, growing 15% monthly, profitable, and bootstrapped. No VC committee approving your budget. No 18-month runway clock ticking in the background.
 



  





**Position Summary** 




 We're looking for a Head of Demand Generation to:
 


* Own the metric. ICP inbound demos booked is what you're measured on, and your success directly determines your upside
* Build the demand engine from strategy through execution, then scale the team around what works
* Operate at the intersection of enterprise marketing and AI, not just using AI tools, but betting on the channels that our legacy software competitors will be too slow to adopt



  





**Your Mission** 




 You'll inherit a team with strong conviction, a product with genuine traction, a few channels that work very, very well, and a budget to deploy.
 



  





 You'll work closely with the CEO, Co-Founder. You will have immense impact over all parts of marketing, from brand, to creative, to socials. It's your oyster. We have ideas. Some will be good, some won't be. It will be your call to say yes or no. You need the judgment to tell the difference, the conviction to push back, and the ability to execute your own strategy when you do.
 



  





 If you've spent the last few years owning a channel and being solely accountable for its outcomes, this is the step up. You'll own the entire function. The upside is uncapped, and so is the responsibility.
 



  





**In your first month you can expect to:** 



* Audit what's working today: where demos are coming from, what's converting, what's being left on the table
* Get deep into the product. Talk to customers and opportunities, you can't market what you don't understand, and our buyers are sophisticated



  





**In your first three months you can expect to:** 



* Have your core channels identified and producing pipeline
* Be running experiments across content, paid, events, or whatever you've identified as highest-leverage
* Have a clear point of view on where to double down and what to kill



  





**In your first six months you can expect to:** 



* Own a repeatable demand engine that's driving consistent inbound demo volume
* See inbound ICP demos improve, month on month. And have plans for how to keep up the growth
* Have a case for your first hire and a plan for how the function scales
* Be deeply embedded in the go-to-market motion alongside sales leadership



  





**What we're looking for:** 



* 3-6 years in B2B marketing with demonstrated ownership of at least one channel end-to-end. You can point to the numbers and say "I did that."
* Experience marketing to enterprise buyers, ideally in B2B SaaS (selling to sales/revenue leaders) or financial services (investor relations personas). You understand long sales cycles and multi-stakeholder buying committees.
* High creative taste. You care about how things look, how copy reads, and whether a campaign actually resonates with a senior buyer. Performance marketing metrics matter, but not at the expense of brand and quality.
* A bootstrapper's instinct for resource allocation. You'll have a real budget, but you think in terms of ROI per dollar, not "spend to plan."
* On the bleeding edge of AI and marketing. Not just using ChatGPT for copy. You have a thesis on which channels and formats are underpriced right now because incumbents haven't caught up.
* Hungry to be an IC who builds. You might want to lead a marketing team at a hyper-growth, profitable SaaS company one day. This is how you get there.



  





**The Culture** 




 We've built a team of people who are genuinely good at what they do. We give direct feedback, ask hard questions, and trust each other to deliver. When things break, we own it and fix it together. When we ship something great, we celebrate.
 



 This isn't a place to coast. But if you want to build something meaningful with people who care as much as you do, you'll love it here.
 



  





**Location & Office** 




 Stockholm: We have an office in Stockholm. We provide standing desks, brand new M3 Macs, 49" Ultra Wide monitors, and licenses to cutting-edge AI tools.
 



  





**Compensation** 




 834,117.82 SEK base + uncapped performance-based compensation tied directly to inbound demo generation. The better you perform, the more you earn, with no ceiling.
 


* Equipment includes latest MacBooks, 49" screens, and any software or AI tools that make you more effective. Monthly team lunches, annual off-sites, and future opportunities to visit our global offices.

title: Acne Studios Global CRM Analyst
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house, and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone's contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimize our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As a CRM Analyst in our Stockholm office, you turn customer data into clear insights that elevate our global CRM efforts. With a strong analytical mindset and curiosity for customer behavior, you deliver reliable reporting, secure data quality, and support the team with insights that drive thoughtful, effective customer engagement.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As CRM Analyst, you bring structure and clarity to our customer initiatives by delivering precise reporting, meaningful insights, and analytical guidance across CRM and clienteling. You ensure data is reliable, follow‑ups are consistent, and key activities are grounded in insight-driven decision‑making by:
   

  

* Produce weekly and monthly CRM reports across global, regional, and store levels
* Ensure accurate, consistent reporting and improve dashboards and templates
* Analyze customer behavior and purchase patterns to uncover insights
* Support segmentation and measure CRM campaign performance
* Drive clienteling insights through KPIs, data checks, and post‑campaign analysis
* Handle ad‑hoc reporting and escalate complex issues when needed
* Maintain clean, validated, and well‑documented customer data
* Collaborate with CRM, retail, and e‑commerce teams to enable insight‑driven decisions


**Who You Are**
 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

**Specific Personal Qualities For This Role**
* Great communication skills, with the ability to explain data clearly and structurally
* Detail‑oriented, structured, and reliable, with the ability to manage multiple requests simultaneously


**Specific Experience And Knowledge For This Role**
* Bachelor's degree in Business, Analytics, Statistics, Engineering, or similar
* 2-3 years' experience as a CRM Analyst or in a related analytical role
* Strong analytical skills and confidence working with data and reporting
* Experience producing regular reports and supporting ad‑hoc analysis
* Solid understanding of CRM fundamentals, customer lifecycle, and segmentation
* Comfortable working in an omnichannel (retail + e‑commerce) environment
* Proficient in tools such as SQL/BigQuery, Excel, Power BI, or similar
* Fluent in English


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.

title: Electronics Design Engineer
description:
**Mission**
 At Neko Health, our mission is to deliver proactive healthcare for all—empowering members to take control of their health through cutting-edge technology and compassionate care.
   

  

 Neko Health is a Swedish healthcare technology company co-founded in 2018 by Hjalmar Nilsonne and Daniel Ek. Neko's vision is to shift healthcare from reactive treatment toward preventative health and early detection. This requires completely reimagining the patient's experience and incorporating the latest advances in sensors and AI.
   

  

 Neko Health has developed a new medical scanning technology concept to make it possible to do broad and non-invasive health data collection that is convenient and affordable for the public. The company is based in Stockholm, offering the Neko Body Scan experience at locations in Stockholm, London and Manchester, with over 500 employees.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for a
 **hands-on Electronics Design Engineer** 
 to join our Hardware & Firmware organization. In this role, you will design and own electronics for sensor-dense medical systems, working closely with Mechanical, Firmware, Software, Verification & Validation, Manufacturing, and Production teams.You will take responsibility across the full product lifecycle — from early requirements and concept development to verification, validation, and production support — with a strong focus on robustness, data quality, and real-world reliability.This is a practical, lab-facing role for engineers who want to see their designs deployed in real clinical environments.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Own electronics design end-to-end, from requirements and concept through schematic, layout, verification, and production support
* Design robust mixed-signal PCBs for sensor-dense medical systems, balancing signal integrity, power, thermal, and EMC constraints
* Select and integrate MCUs / DSPs and key components, working closely with firmware and software teams
* Lead board bring-up, debugging, and root-cause analysis of prototypes and production hardware
* Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure designs are manufacturable, testable, compliant, and scalable


**What You Bring**
* Degree in Electrical Engineering (or equivalent practical experience)
* Minimum 4 years of hands-on electronics product development experience
* Strong experience with mixed-signal PCB design, including schematic, layout, and simulation
* Solid understanding of power design, signal integrity, and EMC/EMI in real products
* Hands-on experience with board bring-up, debugging, and lab instrumentation in embedded systems


**Bonus points**
* Experience working in the medical or other regulated industries
* Experience with RF design or RF-sensitive systems
* Experience developing test interfaces or test systems (e.g. Python, LabVIEW, MATLAB)


**About Titles At Neko**
 We use a simplified internal title framework that prioritises clarity over hierarchy, so internal titles may differ from market‑facing role titles. Scope, impact and level of the role are fully aligned and will be clearly discussed throughout the process.
   

  

**Hiring Process**
 Candidates progress from application and structured screening through thoughtfully designed interviews culminating in a formal offer and final pre-employment checks before joining the team.
   

  

**Equal Opportunity & Inclusion Statement**
 Neko Health is committed to inclusive hiring and member-first care. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage you to request reasonable adjustments to support your application.

title: Technical Field Service Specialist, Hospital Patient Monitoring
description:
Job Title
   

  

 Technical Field Service Specialist, Hospital Patient Monitoring
   

  

**Job Description**
 As a
 **Technical Field Service Specialist** 
 , you will be a key member of the implementation & upgrade project and service team, implementing and upgrading Patient Monitoring solutions at our customer sites. The main goal of your role is to ensure successful implementations & upgrades and positive customer experiences.
   

  

 The majority of this position is targeted geographically within either Storstockholm or north of Sweden, or Region Västra Götaland, but as a natural part of the service organization, travel outside this main focus area will be required, consequently willingness to travel is necessary for this role.
   

  

**Your Role**
* Install, upgrade, deploying, servicing and/or integrating patient monitoring hardware and software solutions.
* Configuring, maintaining, and testing our products at customer sites and/or Philips Delivery Center in Upplands Väsby.
* Technical responsibility for the whole implementation process, from planning through installation & upgrade, go-live and handover.
* Managing detailed quality documentation in adherence to medical directive standards.


**You're The Right Fit If You Have**
* Relevant medical/electrical engineering (or similar) educational background.
* A minimum of 2-3 years of related experience working experience with medical devices and understand the special character working in the hospitals is preferable.
* Have a strong interest and affinity for IT solutions including knowledge on networking (CCNA, CCNP, CWNA, CWTS or equivalent) and IT infrastructures (VMWare, Hyper V, SQL Server) is preferable
* Self-motivated, task-forced team player who is persistent in achieving the goals set.
* Highly professional and knowledgeable approach to solving customer problems and ownership of own projects.
* Fluency in both, Swedish, English and driver's license (Swedish B) and willingness to travel


**How We Work Together**
 We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.
   

  

 Onsite roles require full-time presence in the company’s facilities.
   

  

 Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.
   

  

 This role is classified as a field role.
   

  

**About Philips**
 Philips is a global leader in health technology, committed to improving billions of lives worldwide and striving to make the world healthier and more sustainable through innovation. Driven by the vision of a better tomorrow.
   

  

 But it’s not just what we do, it’s who we are. We are 73,000, wonderfully unique individuals, with two things in common. An unwavering sense of purpose and a relentless determination to deliver on our customers’ needs. It’s what inspires us to create meaningful solutions – the kind that make a real difference – when it matters most.
   

  

 The world and our customers’ needs are changing faster than ever before and while we are proud of what we do already, we know we can do more. That’s why we need you, to help us tackle increasingly complex challenges posed by ever evolving health and well-being needs.
   

  

* Learn more about our business.
* Discover our rich and exciting history.
* Learn more about our purpose.


 If you’re interested in this role and have many, but not all, of the experiences needed, we encourage you to apply. You may still be the right candidate for this or other opportunities at Philips. Learn more about our commitment to diversity and inclusion here. Please kindly apply to the role with a CV in English language

title: Product Manager Leasing B2B
description:
**Full Time/Part Time** 
 Full Time
 



  





**Who We Are** 




 At Ikano Bank, we believe work should feel meaningful and inspiring. Together, we create a great place to work where people are inspired to be the best they can be while contributing to a better life for our customers and the long-term success of the Bank. Guided by our Swedish heritage and values: common sense and simplicity, working together and daring to be different, we keep things straightforward and down-to-earth. If you like the idea of being part of a team that works hard, enjoys solving challenges together and dares to do things a little differently, you will feel right at home here.
 



  





 Digital is the driving force behind the bank’s technology and innovation. We deliver secure, reliable, and competitive products that keep the bank ahead, while enabling growth and expansion into new markets. Digital is not just about keeping systems running—it’s about shaping the future of banking. This is where technology meets opportunity, and where talent drives global impact.
 








**Purpose of the Job** 




 We are looking for an experienced Product Manager who takes full end-to-end ownership of B2B products in a regulated Nordic banking environment. At Ikano Bank, we are a strong player in the Factoring and Leasing segment, operating through direct, partner and broker channels.
 



  





 You thrive at the intersection of business, technology, operations and external vendors, ensuring that products deliver clear customer value across all channels.
