 With Ingka Group as our strong owner, this role offers extensive opportunities to shape and develop the B2B offering within the IKEA product family.
 



  





**Responsibilities of the Job** 



* Support and drive the B2B product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business strategy, customer needs and regulatory requirements
* Lead cross-functional initiatives related to product development, innovation and lifecycle management
* Translate business, customer and regulatory needs into clear, high-quality requirements for internal teams and external vendors
* Collaborate with internal stakeholders to ensure alignment, prioritization and quality of delivery
* Govern and follow up on vendor-led delivery, ensuring scope, quality, dependencies and outcomes are met
* Monitor product performance and conduct product reviews
* Knowing what good market practice is and setting targets
* Ensure product quality through a proactive approach to avoid incidents








**Education Level** 
 Bachelor's Degree
 








**Experience/Demonstrated Competencies** 



* 5+ years of relevant experience within financial services, product management and change initiatives
* Proven track record in B2B product development, preferably within leasing
* Hands-on experience with Jira, Confluence and driving epics from idea to delivery
* Experience working through complex transformations and change programmes
* Strong understanding of BAU operations in a regulated finance context








**Knowledge required** 



* Proven experience as a Product Manager in environments with outsourced services and vendor-led delivery
* Experience owning product backlogs and roadmaps where development and/or operations are handled by third parties
* Solid understanding of B2B banking products, with strong experience in leasing and innovative solutions
* Ability to translate complex business and regulatory requirements into actionable product requirements
* Experience working in regulated Nordic financial services environments, with high demands on compliance, risk and quality
* A structured, delivery-oriented approach with clear focus on outcomes and value realisation








**Capabilities for this job** 
 - B2B Banking and Leasing domain
 


* Product Management & Strategy
* Regulatory, Risk and Quality
* Deliver and Vendor Management
* Collaboration & Stakeholder Management
* Change & Continuous Improvement








**What motivates you?** 
 Collaboration comes naturally to you. You are skilled at aligning internal stakeholders across the bank, as well as working closely with external suppliers, to deliver sustainable and scalable product outcomes. With a strong product mindset and solid business acumen, you own the entire product lifecycle — from strategy and development to implementation, maintenance and continuous improvement.
 



 You are passionate about optimizing products, processes and ways of working, always with the customer at the centre.
 








**Ready to apply?** 




 If this sounds like your kind of workplace, we would love to hear from you. You do not have to tick every single box. What matters most is your drive, your ideas and your willingness to grow with us. Our application process is simple. Just click on the Apply Button and we will get in touch with you soon. Apply today and let us build something different together. You want to know more about Ikano Bank? Check out our videos about culture, social/corporate responsibility and everyday working at the bank. For any questions, feel free to contact us at recruitment@ikano.se.
 








**Salary (per annum)** 
 According to the market
 



**Ikano Bank Benefits** 
 Annual Social Day for all Employees
 



 Favorable Conditions for Ikano Bank Products
 



**Applications Close Date** 
 27 Feb 2026. We review applications on an ongoing basis.

title: Senior Data Scientist - Platform Mission
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
   

  

 The SAFE Insights team supports the five studios that make up the SAFE Alliance: User Platform, User Fraud, IT, Security, and PACE (Procurement, Analytics, and Cost Engineering). We help these studios understand their impact by defining and building performance metrics, running experiments, and delivering analyses that guide decision-making. As a Senior Data Scientist on the team, you’ll collaborate across disciplines to surface insights, shape product thinking, and strengthen a culture of evidence-driven decisions.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Partner with Insights Leads and cross-disciplinary stakeholders to design and build performance metric frameworks.
* Collaborate with data engineers to create pipelines for key metrics and maintain dashboards that support operational and strategic decisions.
* Explore complex datasets to identify patterns, trends, and opportunities that help SAFE studios understand their impact.
* Scope, prioritize, and execute projects including A/B tests and other experimentation approaches.
* Apply statistical and causal inference methods to evaluate product changes for internal and external users.
* Create compelling data stories through visualizations and reporting that influence decision-making across the Alliance.
* Grow into a strategic analytical partner by taking on increasingly complex data challenges.


 Who You Are
   

  

* You know how to frame questions, explore complex datasets, and develop insights that drive clarity and action.
* You are experienced with experimentation, causal inference, and statistical techniques to measure product impact.
* You have a deep understanding of how to communicate insights through dashboards, visualizations, and clear narratives, and are familiar with tools like Tableau, Looker, or similar.
* You are skilled in data science programming languages such as Python or R, and comfortable working with SQL and large-scale data technologies.
* You care about building reliable metrics and helping teams make informed decisions using trustworthy data.
* You have experience collaborating with engineers, product managers, and cross-functional partners.
* You are curious, adaptable, and excited to grow your craft over time.


 Where You'll be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm.
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in-person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.
   

  

 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know – we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Head of AML
description:
Join Froda as our Head of AML, where you’ll shape how we build a smart, risk-based AML capability that supports our European growth journey.
   

  

**About Froda**
 Froda was founded in 2015 with a clear purpose. We want to give small and medium-sized businesses the same opportunities to grow as large corporations. We believe it should be easy for business owners to invest in their ideas, and by democratizing access to financing, we can help more people turn their ambitions into reality.
   

  

 Our business model is based on shared incentives. Therefore, we have no hidden fees, unexpected costs, or surprises, just a transparent partnership built on mutual success. It may sound obvious, but in our industry, it's actually quite rare.
   

  

 We are currently live in 7 markets, 100+ employees, and ready to scale our lending model across Europe.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 As Head of AML, you’ll define Froda’s AML strategy and governance across all markets, ensuring regulatory alignment while enabling the business to move efficiently and safely. You’ll lead the risk-based approach to how AML is woven into product flows, partner journeys, automation, and day-to-day operating model.
   

  

 At Froda, we see the AML function as a strategic enabler, not just a requirement. Therefore, this position is ideal for someone who enjoys working at the intersection of risk, business, tech, and operations. Someone who understands AML deeply and is motivated by supporting business development, helping Froda grow confidently across multiple markets while ensuring we mitigate risk and meet regulatory requirements.
   

  

 Your leadership will ensure AML becomes a trusted, well-understood part of Froda’s operating model, enabling growth without compromising on risk or control.
   

  

**The role includes:**
* Overall responsibility and mandate. Own and refine Froda’s AML/CTF and Sanctions framework, including BWRA, CRR, transaction monitoring, instructions, routines, and processes.
* Translating strategy into actions. Turn strategy and regulatory requirements into actionable tasks.
* Execution and implementation. Ensure progress, delivery and implementation of strategic initiatives and projects.
* Governance and alignment. Define and maintain AML governance, risk appetite, and regulatory alignment across all markets.
* Department and team lead. Lead and develop the AML function and team, building the capabilities and structure required for multi-market operations.
* Internal stakeholder management. Partner with Product, Tech, and Operations to design scalable, automated AML solutions for onboarding, monitoring, and partner journeys.
* Management and board reporting. Act as a trusted advisor to management and the board, ensuring high quality reporting, including current risk, status, and recommended solutions that enables decision-making and direction.
* Support market expansion. Ensuring AML processes, systems, and routines are scalable and ready for new geographies.
* External stakeholder management. Prepare and lead dialogues with regulators, control functions, and other external stakeholders.
* Enable trust-building partnerships. Collaborate and seek possibilities with our partners to drive solutions and integrations that work in symbiosis with our AML frameworks and partner needs.


**What You’ll Bring**
 Your practical
 **work experience and expertise** 
 includes:
   

  

* 5+ years of experience in AML or financial crime prevention, either through senior AML roles or through AML combined with business development or consulting experience.
* Strong understanding of AML regulations and the ability to translate them into business-aligned requirements.
* Leadership experience from building, leading, and developing teams.
* Experience embedding AML into operational workflows, systems, or product flows.
* Holistic mindset, confident judgment and the ability to balance risk management with the overall business objectives and needs.
* Experience building or maturing AML functions in multi-market environments and guiding the business through change.
* Ability to work cross-functionally and influence across Product, Tech, Operations, and Commercial teams.
* University degree in a relevant field.


 To match our mindset of seeing AML not only as a control function, but as a way to enable compliant and scalable growth – we believe your
 **personal skills** 
 include:
   

  

* Ownership and self-leadership. A strong sense of responsibility and self-drive to lead the function, yourself, and team forward, from analysis to implementation.
* Operational excellence mindset. Curiosity to continuously identify and implement risk-based and efficient ways to ensure regulatory requirements are fulfilled.
* Solution-orientation. Suggesting and implementing solutions that balances business objectives with regulatory requirements.
* Decision-making and prioritization. A risk based approach to prioritizing and structuring activities and actions, leading the function towards clear goals, expectations, and project timelines.
* Collaborational skills. You work effectively and build trust with internal functions and external partners, enabling effective collaboration.
* Communication skills. Bringing clarity into complex topics and problems, effectively communicating solutions to the organization.
* Leadership skills. Motivating, developing, and inspiring both the AML team and organization as a whole.


**Application and process**
 We’ll interview candidates on a rolling basis and close the recruitment process as soon as we’ve found the right match – in other words, don’t wait to apply! All candidates will receive an update as soon as selections are made, regardless of outcome. Once you’re in the process, we will keep you updated on the potential stages and timeline.
   

  

 We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Product Manager
description:
Do you want to power a world where people, businesses, and nature thrive together? As a Product Manager, you will be responsible for the full lifecycle of selected hedging products and services that help customers make their projects financially feasible through electricity price hedging. Our solutions play a role in enabling future build‑out of wind, solar and battery assets.
   

  

**About The Role**
 You will drive product development, support customer cases as a subject‑matter expert, and ensure our sales teams are equipped with the right knowledge and tools. Through close collaboration with internal stakeholders and external partners, you will play a key role in shaping Fortum’s hedging offering for B2B customers.
   

  

 Main responsibilities will include but not be limited to:
   

  

* Drive the development and evolution of Fortum’s hedging products, act as a subject‑matter expert in complex customer cases and ensure sales teams have up‑to‑date product knowledge.
* Manage the full product lifecycle, including follow‑up, alignment with development teams, and ensuring a smooth transition from sales to execution.
* Participate in customer projects to ensure offering–customer fit, realistic timelines, and deliver product trainings to internal sales teams.
* Build and maintain strong stakeholder networks — internal and external — gathering insights for the product roadmap and influencing key partners through effective communication skills.


 The position is based either in Espoo, Finland or Solna, Sweden
   

  

**About You**
 We think you have a couple of years relevant work experience, a suitable academic degree (not limited to business or engineering), and an understanding of Nordic energy markets with preferably knowledge of Fortum’s hedging offering (incl. bankability and ESG). You have a natural ability to explain complex concepts, create high‑quality documentation to relevant stakeholders. Experience from product development, customer‑facing work, or energy‑related commercial environments is considered to your advantage. We will value a quick‑learning, proactive team player with strong interpersonal skills, the ability to influence across teams and countries, and preferably wanting to build a strong internal network.
   

  

 Communicating in English is a must, and you will have an advantage if you also know Finnish and/or Swedish as well.
   

  

**We offer you**
 At Fortum, we believe in a better future and want to be involved in shaping it. Our workplace is a safe place, both physically and mentally. You will grow with professional colleagues, being trusted and free to challenge yourself. We offer comprehensive benefits, flexible working hours, and hybrid work model to support your productivity, development and wellbeing. We are committed to build diverse teams where everyone feels included and is treated equally.
   

  

 More information about our culture and benefits can be found here.
   

  

 Espoo office is a smoke-free workplace.
   

  

 For permanent employment with Fortum Sverige AB, you may be assigned a wartime posting within the organization. For further information regarding wartime posting, please refer to the Swedish Civil Defence and Resilience Agency.
   

  

**Interested?**
 Submit your application (CV) through our system by 15th of March 2026, at the latest. We will review applications on an ongoing basis. The position may be filled before last application date.
   

  

 Contact details to union representatives can be found on the bottom of this page.
   

  

 If you would like to hear more about the position, please contact recruitment manager Juha Lounesto via email juha.lounesto@fortum.com. We will review all applications; proactive contact is appreciated. The use of Copilot is encouraged if you encounter new or unfamiliar terms we mention in this job post.
   

  

**We are Fortum**
 Fortum is a Nordic energy company. We generate and deliver reliable energy to our customers and the Nordic energy system while at the same time helping industries decarbonise their processes and grow. Our core operations comprise efficient and best-in-class low-carbon power generation, customer services, and heating and cooling. Fortum’s power generation is already 99% from renewable or nuclear sources with one of the lowest specific CO2-emissions in Europe. We are guided by our ambitious SBTi-validated emission reduction targets on our way towards net-zero by 2040. For our :4,500 employees, we commit to be a safe and inspiring workplace. Fortum's share is listed on Nasdaq Helsinki. fortum.com

title: Staff Software Engineer
description:
***Welcome to Warner Bros. Discovery… the stuff dreams are made of.***








**Who We Are…**





 When we say, “the stuff dreams are made of,” we’re not just referring to the world of wizards, dragons and superheroes, or even to the wonders of Planet Earth. Behind WBD’s vast portfolio of iconic content and beloved brands, are the
 *storytellers* 
 bringing our characters to life, the
 *creators* 
 bringing them to your living rooms and the
 *dreamers* 
 creating what’s next…
 









 From brilliant creatives, to technology trailblazers, across the globe, WBD offers career defining opportunities, thoughtfully curated benefits, and the tools to explore and grow into your best selves. Here you are supported, here you are celebrated, here you can thrive.
 




  


 Warner Bros. has been entertaining audiences for more than 90 years through the world’s most-loved characters and franchises. Warner Bros. employs people all over the world in a wide variety of disciplines. We're always on the lookout for energetic, creative people to join our team.
 




  


**Staff**
**Software Engineer for Cluster Engineering**




  


***Every great story has a new beginning, and yours starts here.***

***Welcome to Warner Bros. Discovery… the stuff dreams are made of.***








 When we say, “the stuff dreams are made of,” we’re not just referring to the world of wizards, dragons and superheroes, or even to the wonders of Planet Earth. Behind WBD’s vast portfolio of iconic content and beloved brands, are the 
 *storytellers* 
  bringing our characters to life, the
 *creators* 
  bringing them to your living rooms and the 
 *dreamers* 
  creating what’s next…
 







 From brilliant creatives, to technology trailblazers, across the globe, WBD offers career defining opportunities, thoughtfully curated benefits, and the tools to explore and grow into your best selves. Here you are supported, here you are celebrated, here you can thrive.
 



  


**Meet our team:** 








 The Direct to Consumer (DTC) Group is a technology company within Warner Bros Discovery. We are building a global streaming video platform (OTT), and a suite of applications to support all of our network’s brands globally. We are building modern container-based micro-services operated on AWS. Our platform covers everything from search, catalogue, video transcoding, personalization, to global subscriptions, and really much more. We build user experiences ranging from classic lean-back viewing to interactive learning applications.  We build for connected TVs, web, mobile phones, tablets, and consoles for a large footprint of WBD owned networks (HBO Max, Discovery, Food Network, Golf TV, MotorTrend, Eurosport, Discovery Play, and many more).  This is a growing, global engineering group crucial to WBD’s future. 
 







 We are hiring a Staff Software Engineer within the DTC Operational Engineering group with a focus on Cluster Engineering. The team provides a secure, efficient and easy-to-use container runtime platform. We build automation for expanding it to new markets, tenants and geographical regions, bootstrapping a fully featured container runtime ready for back-end development teams to use.
 



 We team up with other platform teams, manage the lifecycle of critical platform components and work closely with SRE to improve reliability and efficiency of the platform.
 



 We provide strategies and processes for lifecycle management of the container runtime environment. We own critical components, assess when we should introduce new ones and set the bar for quality. We focus on reducing security incidents while ensuring cost-effectiveness and supporting business needs.
 



  


**Your new role:** 








 As a Staff Software Engineer on the team, you will be a subject matter expert in Kubernetes, helping to define the overall architecture and driving implementations. You will be a strong contributor to improving our internal developer platform, serving thousands of software engineers across the globe.
 



 Tasks include operating a large fleet of Kubernetes clusters, e.g. version upgrades, security patching, cost optimization, scaling and performance tuning. As part of rolling out upgrades to hundreds of clusters, some ranging up to a thousand nodes, you will identify automation opportunities to reduce the amount of toil needed. To keep our runtime cost low, you will lead cost optimization efforts, like tweaking the Karpenter node scaling strategies, increasing the bin-packing efficiency of pods on nodes, ensuring the right node family type is used (m, c, r, spot and Graviton/ARM) and identifying over scaled infrastructure.
 



 To enable faster time to new markets and onboarding of new tenants to our streaming platform, you’ll define automation processes for the creation of new Kubernetes clusters together with bootstrapping of critical platform capabilities like service mesh, deployment systems and the observability stack (metrics, tracing, logging).
 



  


**Preferred Skills:** 







* At least 8 years of software development, infrastructure management or operations experience.
* At least 5 years of Kubernetes and AWS experience.
* Experience with architecture design and developing platform solutions.
* Ability to mentor senior engineers.
* Experience with leading large technical projects with unclear requirements.
* Ability to drive decision making and cross-team collaboration.
* Ability to write quality code for automation in python, bash or golang.
* Experience with designing infrastructure CI/CD pipelines, e.g. Jenkins or GitHub Actions.
* Experience with IaC, preferably Terraform.
* Used to Helm templating for k8s manifests.
* Understanding of how GitOps tooling like ArgoCD or Flux works.
* Experience in rolling out infrastructure changes to production by following a change management workflow.
* Knowing what metrics to monitor during a change rollout to identify problems.
* Strong ownership mentality during rollouts, stop/rollback and fix if problems occur or escalate to management/on-call if getting stuck.
* Strong sense of security, always using least privileges access and firewall configurations when needed for maintenance.
* Understanding of how running workloads on the Kubernetes clusters may be affected by cluster changes or node rotations.
* Willingness to talk to service development teams and understand their challenges when they report problems during maintenance windows.
* Ability to define and measure KPIs and honor SLAs for infrastructure maintenance.
* Experience with Git and GitHub PR workflows.
* Experience in working with Agile – Sprints, Epics/Stories, Jira.
* Experience with building internal developer platforms using technologies such as backstage/argo-workflows



**How We Get Things Done…** 




 This last bit is probably the most important! Here at WBD, our guiding principles are the core values by which we operate and are central to how we get things done. You can find them at www.wbd.com/guiding-principles/  along with some insights from the team on what they mean and how they show up in their day to day. We hope they resonate with you and look forward to discussing them during your interview.
 







**Additional Information** 




 On December 5, Netflix and Warner Bros. Discovery announced they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Netflix will acquire Warner Bros., including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO. The transaction is expected to close after the separation of Warner Bros. Discovery's Global Networks division, Discovery Global, into a new publicly-traded company, which is expected to be completed in Q3 2026. For more details, including leadership appointments and information on individual brands, please visit our newsroom here.
 







 Although you will be hired by Warner Bros. Discovery, upon the planned spin-off of Discovery Global in 2026, your employment likely will transition to Warner Bros. During this period of transformation, you’ll have an exciting opportunity to lay the foundation at one of the world’s premier entertainment brands.
 



















  


**How We Get Things Done…**









 This last bit is probably the most important! Here at WBD, our guiding principles are the core values by which we operate and are central to how we get things done. You can find them at
 












 www.wbd.com/guiding-principles/
 











  
 
 along with some insights from the team on what they mean and how they show up in their day to day. We hope they resonate with you and look forward to discussing them during your interview.
 








**Championing Inclusion at WBD**



 Warner Bros. Discovery embraces the opportunity to build a workforce that reflects a wide array of perspectives, backgrounds and experiences. Being an equal opportunity employer means that we take seriously our responsibility to consider qualified candidates on the basis of merit, regardless of sex, gender identity, ethnicity, age, sexual orientation, religion or belief, marital status, pregnancy, parenthood, disability or any other category protected by law.
 









 If you’re a qualified candidate with a disability and you require adjustments or accommodations during the job application and/or recruitment process, please visit our
 
 accessibility page
 
 for instructions to submit your request.

title: Senior Software Consultant
description:
At Rocket, the belief is that companies exist for people – not the other way around. Now they’re looking for a
 **Senior Developer** 
 to join their dynamic team of consultants and their journey forward!
 



  





 Whether they’re diving into complex new technologies or heading out on their next surfing or skiing adventure, your team at Rocket champions transforming complexity into simplicity and enjoyment. You’ll be working with amazing people and exciting technology, having fun, being innovative, and creating outstanding products and services in areas like automation, full-stack development, cloud services & DevOps, and security.
 



  





 In your role as a Senior Developer, you’ll work on exciting projects for their clients while also having the chance to hone your skills on fun internal projects – which Rocket will happily tell you more about!
 



  





*"The House of Deep Tech expertise within Full Stack Development, Applied AI and Automation"* 




  





**About you** 




 We’re looking for a true tech enthusiast at heart – a self-proclaimed nerd with a lifelong passion for technology. You didn’t pursue education just for its sake but because technology is part of your DNA. Your innate talent for tech stands out – you have a knack for diving deep into new technologies and quickly getting up to speed.
 



  





**You have:** 



* At least
 **five years of experience** 
 as a front- or backend developer and are ready to take the next step in your career
* Proficiency in one or more
 **high-level programming languages or frameworks** 
 , such as Python, Java, C/C++/C#, .NET, JavaScript, Ruby, React, Angular, Node, Vue, or Bootstrap
* Solid experience
 **designing and developing REST APIs**
* Good knowledge of
 **cloud technologies** 
 , such as App Services and Functions
* Experience working with modern
 **CI/CD** 
 pipelines using tools like
 **GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps,** 
 or
 **GitLab CI**
* Strong skills in
 **software architecture, design, implementation, and deployment** 
 , which will be invaluable to the team and future projects
* **Ability to work onsite** 
 in the greater Stockholm metropolitan area (this is not a remote position)
* **Fluency in both Swedish and English**



  





 To top it off, we see it as a bonus if you have experience as a consultant, design skills (HTML5, CSS, SCSS, SVG, dynamic layouts, etc.), knowledge in RPA and automation, experience in machine learning, or database expertise.
 



  





**What can Rocket offer you?** 



* **Partnership** 
 🤝 – Influence the company’s future, participate in internal development projects, and even have the opportunity for future ownership.
* **Innovation** 
 💡 – Rocket loves innovation and working on internal development projects.
* **Occupational Health Services** 
 🌡️ – Comprehensive health care coverage.
* **Exciting Conferences** 
 🌍 – Fantastic adventure conferences and getaways with colleagues.
* **Flexibility** 
 🏡 – Rocket is highly flexible. A typical week includes a few days at the client site, one or two days in the office, and one day working from home.
* **Social Gatherings** 
 🥳 – Social events to connect with colleagues.
* **Central Office** 
 🏢 – A startup-style office located centrally for your convenience, close to public transport, just a minute from Östermalmstorg station.



  





**Performance, Kindness & Trust - Rocket** 




  





*Rocket collaborates with Safemind for this recruitment.*

title: Strategic Account Manager
description:
As a Strategic Account Manager at Upsales, you operate where technology, customer value, and long-term growth meet. Your mission is to help existing customers realize the full potential of Upsales by translating AI capabilities into measurable outcomes across their revenue operations.
   

  

 Upsales is not just a CRM. We are Europe’s leading AI platform for B2B revenue acceleration. Founded in Stockholm and built for high-performing revenue teams, we help ambitious companies work smarter, predict better, and scale with precision. Our culture is shaped by ownership, craftsmanship, and an AI-first mindset.
   

  

 In this role, you work cross-functionally with Solutions Engineers, Onboarding, and Customer Success to deeply understand each customer’s setup, goals, and challenges. You act as a trusted advisor, connecting product capabilities with real-world use cases. This is about long-term value creation, not transactional selling.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Take ownership of a strategic portfolio of existing customers with a focus on adoption, value realization, and sustainable expansion
* Develop a deep understanding of customer workflows, data maturity, and business objectives
* Identify opportunities where Upsales’ AI and platform capabilities can unlock new value
* Co-create solution designs and success plans together with Solutions Engineers and Customer Success
* Lead strategic account dialogues, roadmap discussions, and executive-level conversations
* Drive expansion through demonstrated impact, not pressure or volume
* Collaborate with Product, Customer Success, and Sales leadership to continuously improve how we deliver customer value
* Contribute to ARR growth by helping customers scale successfully with Upsales over time


**AI first mindset**
 We’re not looking for someone who simply uses AI. We want someone who weaponizes it. You treat tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI copilots as force multipliers, allowing you to deliver at a level and speed others can’t match. For you, AI isn’t an add-on, it’s the default operating system for how you think, work, and lead. You’re relentlessly curious about new capabilities, and you push the limits of what’s possible, turning AI into a competitive advantage for yourself, Upsales, and our customers. This is a role for someone with real ambition: someone who wants to set a new standard for efficiency, precision, and impact in the age of AI.
   

  

 Who We’re Looking For
   

  

* At least 5 years of proven experience in complex B2B sales
* A strong track record of meeting and exceeding targets
* Experience building long-term relationships with fast-growing mid-sized companies
* A proactive, ambitious mindset with full ownership of the sales process
* Excellent communication skills in both English and Swedish
* Driven by growth, responsibility, and the ambition to build something meaningful


 You are not looking for just another role. You are looking for momentum.
   

  

 Upsales is a bootstrapped company with big ambitions, known for our high-performing team, relentless curiosity, and a culture that challenges conventional thinking. From product to sales, we’re obsessed with creating real value for our customers and building software that actually moves the needle.

title: Frontendutvecklare med intresse för Python
description:
Vill du vara en del av en dynamisk arbetsplats där du tillsammans med kompetenta kollegor verkligen gör skillnad för ett hållbart och lysande Sverige. Då kan det här vara jobbet för dig.
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 I rollen som frontend-utvecklare med intresse för Python ansvarar du främst för att utveckla och vidareutveckla interna applikationer för analys av kraftsystem och elmarknad. Systemen hanterar stora datamängder och används för planering av det svenska transmissionsnätet på kort och lång sikt. Arbetet sker i ett tvärfunktionellt agilt team och i en miljö med höga säkerhetskrav, där vi huvudsakligen arbetar med on-prem-lösningar.
   

  

 Tjänsten kommer att innefatta att:
   

  

* Utveckla och vidareutveckla applikationer i bland annat React och Python
* Bidra till arkitektur, design och tekniska vägval inom givna säkerhetsramar
* Arbeta fullstack med tyngdpunkt på frontend, samt backend vid behov
* Skriva och genomföra tester för att säkerställa kvalitet och stabilitet
* Hantera incidenter och delta i drift- och stabiliseringsarbete
* Konfigurera och underhålla CI/CD-flöden i Azure DevOps Server och GitLab
* Framtagning och uppdatering av systemdokumentation, exempelvis teknisk dokumentation, arkitekturbeskrivningar och användarstöd


 Du får möjlighet att utvecklas inom systemutveckling i komplexa och säkerhetsklassade miljöer, arbeta med moderna verktyg som React, Python och containerteknik samt bidra i uppbyggnaden av nya system där arkitektur och teknikval fortfarande formas. Tillsammans med din enhetschef planerar du din kompetensutveckling och har tillgång till både interna och externa utbildningsresurser. Du får också utbyta erfarenheter med kollegor med lång erfarenhet inom IT och kraftsystemanalys.
   

  

 Som frontend-utvecklare tillhör du avdelningen IT för Kraftsystem & elmarknad, sektion IT för Kraftsystemanalys. Enheten består av cirka 19 medarbetare och arbetar agilt i både projekt och förvaltning. Teamet består av kravanalytiker, scrum master, systemutvecklare och testare och samarbetar nära produktägare och kraftsystemanalytiker.
   

  

**Om dig**
**Skallkrav:**
 Vi söker dig som har ett analytiskt förhållningssätt och kan sätta dig in i komplexa problem kopplade till stora datamängder och tekniska begränsningar. Du arbetar resultatorienterat och tar ansvar för att driva dina uppgifter framåt. För att trivas i rollen ser vi att du är samarbetsinriktad och trivs i ett tvärfunktionellt team där dialog med kollegor och användare är en naturlig del av arbetet. Du behöver även vara kreativ och kunna hitta lösningar inom givna säkerhets- och arkitekturramar.
   

  

 Du ska också leva upp till den statliga värdegrunden. Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Vidare har du en examen inom om minst 180 hp inom systemvetenskap, IT, eller ingenjörsutbildning eller motsvarande kompetens förvärvad på annat sätt som Svenska kraftnät bedömer som likvärdig.
   

  

 Du har även flera års arbetslivserfarenhet av:
   

  

* Frontendutveckling med moderna språk och ramverk, till exempel React, Angular, Next.js eller liknande och ett intresse av att jobba med React.
* Agila arbetssätt
* CI/CD och DevOps‑miljöer med verktyg som exempelvis Azure DevOps Server, GitLab och/eller GitHub
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska


 Vi ser det som meriterande om du har arbetslivserfarenhet av:
   

  

* Backendutveckling i Python
* Python-bibliotek för dataanalys till exempelvis Pandas
* Testautomatisering eller konfiguration av CI/CD-pipelines
* Systemutveckling av applikationer som stödjer dataanalys, nätberäkningsprogram eller annan kraftsystemanalys
* projekt där ISO 27001 har tillämpats
* Containerteknik, såsom Kubernetes eller OpenShift
* Systemutveckling inom kraftsystemsektorn, eller arbete i en samhällsviktig och säkerhetskänslig myndighet


**Bra att veta**
* Tjänsten är placerad i Stockholm / Sundbyberg
* Möjlighet till distansarbete upp till 50 %
* Sista ansökningsdag är den 19 mars, vi använder urvalsfrågor istället för personligt brev. Vänligen bifoga ditt CV på svenska som tydligt beskriver den erfarenhet som vi söker
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning
* I denna rekryteringsprocess tillämpas arbetspsykologiska tester
* Förmåner | Svenska kraftnät


 I denna rekrytering samarbetar vi med Ada Digital. Vid frågor kontakta rekryteringskonsult Sandra Peiper, sandra.peiper@adadigital.se. Vid frågor om rekryteringsprocessen kontakta ansvarig rekryterare hos Svenska kraftnät Maria Sahlén, 010-489 20 27 (maria.sahlen2@svk.se). Fackliga representanter är Annika Ingeborn, SACO, 010-475 87 72 och Erica Midfjäll, ST, 010-475 87 31. Du når oss också via e-post: fornamn.efternamn@svk.se
   

  

 Jobba här – bli en viktig del av vår energiframtid | Svenska kraftnät
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare i samband med rekrytering, vi har upphandlade avtal som gäller för Svenska kraftnät som statlig myndighet.
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) att genomföras i de fall befattningen innebär hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerade uppgifter eller deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet. Om anställningen avser befattning i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövningen även att innefatta en registerkontroll och, i vissa fall, en särskild personutredning. För anställning på Svenska kraftnät kan det vara ett krav på svenskt medborgarskap i vissa befattningar. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. För mer information om säkerhetsprövning Svenska kraftnät arbetar för att främja mångfald och motverka diskriminering och ser gärna sökanden med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter till våra rekryteringar. Lär känna Svenska kraftnät och hur det är att arbeta hos oss: Lär känna oss på Svenska kraftnät

title: Infrastrukturspecialist Backup
description:
Svenska kraftnät är en nyckelaktör i energiomställningen. Vår IT-division växer för att möjliggöra en fördubblad elproduktion och en fossilfri framtid. Med den senaste tekniken ökar vi både infrastrukturens kapacitet och utvecklingstakten inom digitalisering och processer, för fortsätta resan framåt söker vi nu en Infrastrukturspecialist till Svenska kraftnät.
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 Som Infrastukturspecialist kommer du att tillhöra enheten Databas och Lagring inom avdelningen Infrastruktur och Drift. I ditt team har du duktiga infrastukturspecialister och arkitekter inom lagring och databas som utvecklar och underhåller Svenska kraftnäts lagring- och databasmiljöer.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att ansvara och arbeta med utveckling och underhåll av vårt befintliga och kommande backupsystem. Du kommer även att vara drivande och bidra med att utveckla vår kommande backuplösning. Här ges du möjlighet att forma hur backup-plattformen ska användas inom ramar för verksamheten.
   

  

 Arbetet innefattar även drift, installation, konfiguration, och övervakning av backup-plattform, samt att felsöka och återställa data vid behov. För att säkerställa kontinuitet i verksamheten är en central uppgift att utveckla och implementera strategier för backup och återläsning.
   

  

 Kring den nya plattformen kommer du att arbeta med att sprida kunskap och kommunicera om den nya backuplösningen till IT på Svenska kraftnät. Du ansvarar för att tydliggöra nya arbetssätt och dess fördelar samt stöttar verksamheten i övergången till den nya lösningen. Eftersom lösningen kommer att användas brett i hela verksamheten är rollen central för att säkerställa god förståelse, korrekt användning och en effektiv migrering.
   

  

 Tjänsten kommer också innefatta att
   

  

* Vara rådgivande och delaktig i ny design och arkitektur för backuplösning.
* Dokumentera backuplösningen med tillhörande arbetsinstruktioner.
* Implementera och underhålla samt utveckla Svenska kraftnäts nya Backuplösning.
* Kompetensöverföring och sprida kunskap om ny backuplösning inom IT.
* Assistera med återläsningstester och verifiera återhämtningsplaner.
* Följa interna processer för ärendehantering.


 IT-verksamheten på Svenska kraftnät är i ständig utveckling och vi arbetar med att förbättra tekniska lösningar såväl som arbetsmetoder och processer. I rollen ingår även att delta i den typen av utvecklingsaktiviteter, och du kommer att ges goda möjligheter att utvecklas, bland annat genom utbildningar och erfarenhetsutbyte med kollegor som arbetat länge inom branschen.
   

  

**Om dig**
**Skallkrav:**
 I denna roll är det mycket viktigt att du har god samarbetsförmåga. Du ska kunna relatera till andra på ett lyhört sätt och kunna kommunicera konstruktivt och tydligt. Du har sannolikt erfarenhet av att arbeta i en större, mer komplex organisation och gärna i en miljö under förändring. Du trivs med att arbeta resultatinriktat med ditt team och verksamheten, ofta i nära samarbete med medarbetare från andra delar av organisationen. Din framgång kommer att vila mycket på din förmåga att ta initiativ, ställa rätt frågor, lyssna in och dra slutsatser. Det är naturligt för dig att ta ansvar och se att ditt och teamets uppdrag är en del av ett större sammanhang. Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Du har relevant utbildning inom IT alternativt flerårig arbetslivserfarenhet inom området som Svenska kraftnät bedömer som likvärdig. Du har även:
   

  

* Minst 5 års relevant och aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet inom backupsystem Commvault Backup & Recovery eller motsvarande system.
* Grundläggande kunskaper inom nät och serverinfrastruktur samt operativsystem som Windows och Linux.
* Körkort
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska.


 Det är
 **meriterande** 
 om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av att sätta upp Commvault mot Enterprise-applikationer.
* God kännedom om lagringssystem.
* Erfarenhet i att arbeta i en förvaltningsroll med livscykelhantering, patch-hantering och proaktivitet.
* Erfarenhet av att ha arbetat och etablerat lösning i miljö med höga säkerhetskrav.
* Intresse för automatisering.
* Erfarenhet av container-lösningar.
* Vi ser gärna att du sedan tidigare har arbetat med komplexa miljöer samt har erfarenhet av ITIL-processer och ärendehantering.


**Bra att veta**
* Tjänsten är placerad Sundbyberg
* Möjlighet till distansarbete upp till 50 %
* Sista ansökningsdag är 2026-04-07. Vi använder urvalsfrågor istället för personligt brev.
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning
* Beredskap ingår i tjänsten
* Förmåner | Svenska kraftnät


**Intresserad av att ansöka eller få veta mer?**
 I denna rekrytering samarbetar Svenska kraftnät med Computer Sweden Recruitment. För frågor kring tjänsten och rekryteringsprocessen, välkommen att kontakta huvudansvarig rekryteringskonsult, Johanna Nordberg, 070-33 43 159, johanna.nordberg@csrecruitment.se eller rekryterare hos Svenska kraftnät, Victoria Waltenberg. Fackliga representanter är Annika Ingeborn, SACO, 010 475 87 72 och Erica Midfjäll ST, 010 475 87 31. Du når oss också via e-post: fornamn.efternamn@svk.se
   

  

 Jobba här – bli en viktig del av vår energiframtid | Svenska kraftnät
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare i samband med rekrytering, vi har upphandlade avtal som gäller för Svenska kraftnät som statlig myndighet.
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) att genomföras i de fall befattningen innebär hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerade uppgifter eller deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet. Om anställningen avser befattning i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövningen även att innefatta en registerkontroll och, i vissa fall, en särskild personutredning. För anställning på Svenska kraftnät kan det vara ett krav på svenskt medborgarskap i vissa befattningar. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. För mer information om säkerhetsprövning Svenska kraftnät arbetar för att främja mångfald och motverka diskriminering och ser gärna sökanden med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter till våra rekryteringar. title: Solution Sales Expert, CX
description:
**We help the world run better**
 At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed.
   

  

**WHO ARE WE?**
 SAP Customer Experience (CX) is a line of business organization (LoB) as part of a matrix sales structure. CX belongs to one of the top three strategic focuses of SAP globally and is major contributor to the growth in SAPs global cloud business. The CX solution portfolio is the most comprehensive in the market (a leader in analysts’ reports and quadrants in the areas we compete in) and enables companies of all sizes to manage their customers in today’s demanding customer centric environment. It is often used to transform the business into the digital world covering processes in Digital Commerce, Customer Data Management, Marketing, Sales and Service and goes beyond traditional CRM.
   

  

 You will be part of an energetic team and be working in a high pace environment. CX is one of the fastest growing and most innovative Line of Business within SAP, and with a world evolving quickly around us and with customers in need of changing the way they to deliver a superior customer experience, it is time for us to find Senior Solution Sales Executive to our CX team in Sweden. You will own and manage a set of top accounts (territory), and work in close collaboration with SAP Industry sales and a wider Virtual Account Team.
   

  

**EXPECTATIONS AND TASKS**
 Based in the Stockholm office you will take the full ownership of your territory, driving the sales opportunities from early discovery phase to closing. You will furthermore drive and support demand generation activities, enablement and thought leadership to drive SAP CX solutions in our region.
   

  

* You will be the CX lighthouse in the industry you work for – responsible for shaping next generation CX in close collaboration with your customers.
* You are passionate about CX as a domain, and are mainly focused on customer stakeholders owning the business responsibility of further developing the CX domains (such as CDO’s, Chief Sales Officers, Heads of Service etc.)
* Actively understand each customer’s technology footprint, strategic growth plans, technology strategy and competitive landscape
* You will own and drive customer success via a healthy pipeline and work in a Virtual Account Teams from CX


**WHO ARE YOU?**
* You have a 10 years experience working with sales in the Customer Experience Space (CRM/Sales, eCommerce, Marketing, Service, Customer Data Management) and/or other business software
* You have a proven track record in business application cloud sales
* You have demonstrated success with large transactions and lengthy sales cycles in a fast-paced, consultative, and competitive market.
* You a fluent in local language and English, and are able to make our customers feel listened to and help them with their needs
* You embrace a continuous learning attitude


 To be successful in this position, it is crucial that you possess strong organizational skills to drive complex technology go-to-market within a highly agile and changing business environment. You are an excellent internal & external communicator and you hold great presentation skills to clearly articulate the business value and differentiation of the CX solution portfolio. You can anticipate change, possess innovative business acumen and are an "out of the box" thinking person to guide execution in a fast-paced environment.
   

  

 #CX
   

  

**Bring out your best**
 SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.
   

  

**We win with inclusion**
 SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.
   

  

 SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.
   

  

 For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.
   

  

 Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements.
   

  

 Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor.
   

  

**AI Usage in the Recruitment Process**
 For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process.
   

  

 Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.

title: AWS DevOps Engineer
description:
**Description**
 They say, ‘it’s not rocket science.’ We disagree.
   

  

 Welcome to SSC Space (formerly Swedish Space Corporation), the perfect workplace for those passionate about technology in general and space in particular.
   

  

 With space organizations, research institutes, institutional and commercial actors as customers, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications, space consulting services, rocket and balloon systems, flight test services, satellite propulsion systems, and much more.
   

  

 SSC also has one of the world’s largest global networks of ground stations for satellite communications. We own and operate the Esrange Space Center in Kiruna, northern Sweden, with the aim of launching satellites in the near future.
   

  

 At SSC Space, our systems help launch rockets and control satellites. To do that, we need robust automation, reliable deployments, and a cloud platform that just works. That's why we are looking for an AWS DevOps Engineer who thrives on clean pipelines, codified infrastructure, and high uptime.
   

  

**Requirements**
 As AWS DevOps Engineer, you’ll be a builder of automation, an advocate for reliability, and a champion for operational excellence and with a focus on Observability. You’ll work hands-on with AWS and modern DevOps tools to streamline how we deploy and manage our systems.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Develop and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform or CloudFormation
* Design and operate monitoring stacks (e.g. Grafana, CloudWatch)
* Define logging, metrics, and tracing standards
* Build CI/CD pipelines for cloud-based systems and applications
* Automate deployment and environment provisioning workflows
* Manage containerized workloads with EKS, ECS, or Fargate
* Monitor systems and respond to operational incidents
* Improve reliability through observability, testing, and automation
* Collaborate with developers and architects on solution design and deployment
* Document infrastructure and processes for internal use and audits
* Quality assurance
* Software supply chain – ECR, JFROG
* Building policy engine and guardrails focus on security
* Application Performance Monitoring “observability”.


**Responsibilities**
 We're looking for someone who loves automation and takes pride in reliable systems. You’re curious about how things work and committed to continuous improvement.
   

  

**You Have**
* 3+ years of hands-on AWS experience in production environments
* Solid skills in infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, CloudFormation)
* Experience building CI/CD pipelines (e.g. GitHub Actions, AWS CodePipeline, Jenkins)
* Familiarity with container orchestration and Linux system administration
* Knowledge of monitoring and logging solutions in AWS and Grafana.


**Valuable additional experience:**
* Experience with Ansible or similar config management tools
* Scripting skills (Python, Bash)
* Exposure to DevOps practices.


**How you work** 
 :
   

  

* Automation-first mindset
* Collaborative and service-minded
* Comfortable working across IT and development teams
* Fluent in Swedish and English.


**Benefits**
 WE OFFER YOU
   

  

* A unique opportunity to contribute to a wide range of projects and play an active role in both the Swedish and international space industry.
* An excellent opportunity for personal and career development.
* An exciting, international workplace characterized by an open atmosphere and proud employees all over the world.


**Additional Benefits**
* In addition to vacation and traditional public holidays, we offer extra paid leave throughout the year, such as time off between Christmas and New Year, Ascension Day, amounting to 6-11 extra days depending on the calendar.
* Wellness allowance of SEK 5,000 per year.
* Health benefits such as voluntary health insurance and regular health checks for eligible employees.
* Access to a benefit bike through a tax-efficient leasing program.
* Support for employee-driven activities that promote connection, health, and culture across teams.
* Collective Agreement, including good pensions plans, parental pay and insurance.


 LOCATION
   

  

 This is a full-time, permanent position with hybrid work setup in our office in Solna Strand.
   

  

 FURTHER INFORMATION
   

  

 For questions regarding employment terms or union-related matters, you are welcome to contact our union representatives:
   

  

 Sveriges Ingenjörer: SverigesIngenjorer-AF@sscspace.com
   

  

 Unionen: unionen@sscspace.com
   

  

  

  

 NEXT STEP
   

  

 If this opportunity sparks your interest, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible! We review applications continuously and will conduct interviews throughout the process.
   

  

 You can submit your application up until 2026-03-29, which is the latest possible date for consideration.
   

  

 Please note, however, that the position may be filled before this date, and the advertisement may therefore close earlier. This allows us to manage the process efficiently and give each application the attention it deserves.
   

  

 When applying, please include your CV. While you are welcome to include a brief personal note or letter, our evaluation will primarily focus on your skills and qualifications as outlined in your CV, ensuring a fair and unbiased recruitment process.
   

  

 This position requires approved pre-employment screening. These controls are based on the role that you will fulfill within the company. Additional requirements regarding citizenship may apply.
   

  

 WE HELP EARTH BENEFIT FROM SPACE
   

  

 SSC Space is a leading global provider of advanced space services, with more than 50 years of experience. We help space organizations, research institutes, commercial and institutional actors from all over the world to get access to space.
   

  

 With local presence on all continents and about 700 committed employees, we offer specialist expertise in satellite communications and satellite control services, spacecraft operations, rocket and balloon systems, launch services and flight test services, as well as engineering, operations and consultancy services for space missions.
   

  

 We help Earth benefit from Space. Learn more at sscspace.com

title: Capital Projects Senior Manager
description:
***Do you want to make an impact - not just control projects, but change how the world gets built?***


 At Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects, you’ll do exactly that. You’ll help develop and deliver the data centers, energy infrastructure, grids, and public infrastructure that keep communities moving - and make it smarter, safer, and more sustainable than ever before.
   

  

 You’ll work alongside people who think big and act bold; project managers, engineers, technologists, and strategists who blend real-world experience with digital innovation and AI. Together, we’re transforming how capital projects are planned, managed, and executed, creating a better way to build for the future.
   

  

 Visit us here to learn more about Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects.
   

  

 The demand for our services is rapidly growing, and we are looking for Capital Projects Senior Managers that can lead and drive our Capital Projects services for our Nordic clients. We are also seeking individuals who have expertise in Contract and Commercial Management, Permit Management, or Cost Management.
   

  

**Your Role**
 As a Capital Projects Senior Manager you will represent and support asset owners in the planning and execution of large, complex capital projects, ensuring strong governance, commercial control, and performance transparency.
   

  

**You will:**
- Lead full lifecycle over from early planning and procurement through construction and close-out

- Act as a trusted advisor to senior client stakeholders

- Drive cost, schedule, risk, and change management discipline

- Oversee contractor coordination, commercial negotiations, and contract compliance

- Ensure regulatory and permitting alignment

- Implement structured reporting and modern project controls

- Lead and develop multidisciplinary teams

- Contribute to business development and strengthen long-term client relationships

 You will play a key role in strengthening and expanding our Infrastructure & Capital Projects practice in Sweden.
   

  

**Who We’re Looking For**
 You are a senior capital project leader with strong commercial judgment and delivery experience.
   

  

**You bring:**
- Extensive experience in large-scale construction or infrastructure projects (owner-side experience is an advantage)

- Strong understanding of contract management, cost control, risk management and project governance

- Experience managing complex stakeholder environments

- Confidence engaging senior decision-makers

- Ability to combine strategic perspective with hands-on execution

- Master’s degree in Engineering, Construction management, Architecture, Law, Business, or similar

- Extensive relevant experience may compensate for formal education

 Experience from capital investment projects within data centers, energy infrastructure, or large public investments is highly valued.
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm or Gothenburg. Some on-site presence is required.
   

  

**Why Join Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects?**
- Work with high-impact infrastructure and capital investments in the Nordics

- Combine capital project expertise with digital, data, and global capabilities

- Work in a collaborative and ambitious environment

- Access continuous learning, certifications, and international expertise

- Competitive compensation and clear career progression

 If you are ready to take ownership of complex infrastructure programs and help grow a leading capital projects practice, we would like to hear from you. Kindly note that legal right to work in Sweden required.
   

  

 Apply now and attach your CV and Cover Letter in English. We look forward to receiving your application! Please submit your application by the end of March.
   

  

 If further questions, feel free to reach out to annika.venalainen@accenture.com (HR/Recruiting).

title: Raw Material Production Manager
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone’s contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimise our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As Raw Material Production Manager in our Stockholm office, you ensure smooth and timely raw material production that supports our global collections. With a structured mindset and strong supplier management skills, you secure material availability, uphold quality, and contribute to sustainability while supporting teams across development and production.
   

  

 This is a temporary position starting mid April 2026 to mid March 2027.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

 WHAT YOU WILL DO
   

  

 As Raw Material Production Manager, your main contribution will be to manage production planning, raw material procurement, and supplier negotiations to ensure timely and high‑quality material deliveries. You will support sustainability initiatives and collaborate closely with Quality and Production teams. You will report to the Senior Raw Material Manager by:
   

  

* Managing global production planning and securing on‑time delivery for bulk materials
* Managing scarf production and ensuring high‑quality deliveries according to drop schedules
* Leading supplier negotiations and overseeing seasonal and annual production calendars
* Ensuring materials meet technical and quality requirements from development to sampling
* Monitoring raw material risks, supporting pre‑booking analysis, and evaluating lead‑time constraints
* Supporting sustainability initiatives, leftover material strategy, and warehousing solutions
* Aligning closely with Production and Quality teams on testing, delivery timelines, and supplier performance


 WHO YOU ARE
   

  

 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

 Specific personal qualities for this role:
   

  

* Actively seeks input from team members to solve challenges collaboratively
* Takes initiative and identifies potential issues early
* Proactive, structured, and strong in follow‑through
* Diligent and persistent in driving tasks and projects to completion
* Understands team workflows and can delegate or oversee tasks effectively
* Builds and maintains strong relationships with suppliers and external partners
* Driven by accountability and reliability


 Specific experience and knowledge for this role:
   

  

* Strong proficiency in MS Office, especially Excel
* Solid understanding of quality and production processes
* Experience with PLM systems is a plus
* Strong costing and negotiation skills
* Excellent communication skills in written and spoken English


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.

title: Fintech Co-Founder / CCO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.
 


  


 We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.
 


  


 Our offer:
 


  

  
* A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to
 **€500k** 
 in funding.
  
* **1:1 sparring with unicorn founders** 
 on a weekly basis
  
* **Community** 
 : Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors
  
* **Team building** 
 : Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)
  
* **Distribution** 
 : Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine
  
* **Funding support** 
 for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)
  


  


 One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.
 


  

 Tasks
   

  
* You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Fintech
  
* You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management
  
* You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs
  
* You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding
  
* You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively
  


  

 Requirements
   

  
* You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate
  
* You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues
  
* You have excellent communication skills in the English language
  


  


 Join us and build a €1B+ company with us!

title: Account Executive, LE/GE, GTS
description:
**About Gartner:**
 Are you looking for a fast-paced career with outstanding advancement potential at a global market leader? If so, think about Gartner. The world's leading research and advisory company, Gartner (NYSE:IT) delivers the technology-related insight necessary for our clients to make the right business decisions, every day. We are seeking highly motivated, achievement-driven sales associates to contribute to our double-digit growth, backed by the solid infrastructure of a world-class sales organization. In addition to a competitive base salary, uncapped commissions and exceptional benefits, Gartner sales associates can enjoy generous performance-based rewards, including all expenses paid trips to exotic locations around the world. Come be a part of our incredible growth organization!
   

  

**Position Summary:**
 The Account Executive is a field-based, direct sales role responsible for both client retention as well as growth through contract expansion and the introduction of new products and services. You will consult with C-level executives to develop and implement an effective, enterprise-wide strategy that maximizes the value delivered by Gartner’s products and services.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Account management with an outcome of increased customer satisfaction and an increase in retention and account growth
* Mastery and consistent execution of Gartner’s sales methodology
* Account planning and territory management
* Managing forecast accuracy on a monthly/quarterly/annual basis
* Maintaining competitive knowledge and focus
* In-depth knowledge of Gartner’s products and services


**Requirements:**
* 6-10 years of experience with proven consultative sales, preferably in high technology (services, software, or consultative environment), with evidence of prior success in Sales
* Strong demonstration of intellect, drive, executive presence and sales acumen
* Proven experience building excellent client relationships at C-level within large enterprise organisations
* Strong computer proficiency and presentation skills
* Professional level of Swedish
* Knowledge of the full life cycle of the sales process
* Bachelors or Masters degree – desired


**What Gartner gives you:**
* Market leading earnings potential
* Unbeatable training and growth opportunities, including an intensive 3-week sales academy for new hires
* Excellent corporate benefits
* The opportunity to sell at C-level and work with some of the brightest minds in the industry


 Your application and the data therein are received and processed by Gartner UK Ltd., which is operating as shared HR service provider for the EMEA entities of the Gartner group. Your application will be shared by Gartner UK Ltd. with those persons within the Gartner group which decide about the steps of your application.
   

  

**Who are we?**
 At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.
   

  

 Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.
   

  

 Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 21,000 associates globally who support ~14,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.
   

  

**What makes Gartner a great place to work?**
 Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.
   

  

 We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.
   

  

 Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.
   

  

 We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.
   

  

**What do we offer?**
 Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.
   

  

 In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.
   

  

 Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.
   

  

 The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.
   

  

 Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com.
   

  

 Job Requisition ID:106361
   

  

 By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the country or regional recruitment notice linked below applicable to your place of residence.
   

  

 Gartner Applicant Privacy Link: https://jobs.gartner.com/applicant-privacy-policy
   

  

**For efficient navigation through the application, please only use the back button within the application, not the back arrow within your browser.**

title: ServiceNow GRC konsult
description:
*Är du en skicklig och ambitiös konsult med erfarenhet av risk- och säkerhetshantering? Har du en passion för teknik och vill hjälpa till att leverera innovativa lösningar via ServiceNow-plattformen? Då vill vi att du ska bli en del av teamet!*
 \_ \_ \_ \_
   

  

**Om oss**
 Risk & Compliance Consulting (RCC) på KPMG Advisory är ett mångsidigt team på cirka 60 kollegor med ämnes expertis inom risktransformation, regelefterlevnad, forensiska utredningar och hantering av bedrägeririsker, intern kontroll, riskhantering, internrevision och hållbarhet. Vi arbetar med ett brett spektrum av riskrådgivningstjänster och våra medarbetare har mycket olika bakgrunder, inklusive ekonomi, juridik, teknik, företagsekonomi och statsvetenskap.
   

  

 Vår mångfald i både människor och kompetenser gör oss till en ledande rådgivare på marknaden. Vi arbetar med högpresterande kunder inom alla sektorer, inklusive privat sektor, offentlig sektor och finansiella tjänster. En viktig del av vårt arbete är digitalisering och integrering av ny teknik för att förändra riskhanterings- och efterlevnadsprocesser för våra kunder. Vi ger råd till våra kunder i deras transformationsresor och främjar digitala, innovativa och hållbara lösningar på våra kunders affärsutmaningar.
   

  

 Vi hjälper våra kunder med rådgivning och implementering av den ledande teknikplattformen ServiceNow, för digitalisering och transformation inom våra discipliner. Våra verksamhetsområden inkluderar riskhantering, säkerhet, supply chain, HR, portföljhantering, leverantörsstyrning och utveckling av kundspecifika lösningar.
   

  

 KPMGs ServiceNow satsning är en global framgångssaga och vårt fokus på professionell expertis och domänexpertis har gjort oss till en föredragen partner, både för ServiceNow och för våra kunder. Våra insatser har uppmärksammats globalt genom att bland annat ha utsetts till ServiceNow Global Transformation Partner of the Year 2023.
   

  

 Vi finns representerade vid KPMGs kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö.
   

  

**Din roll**
 Vi söker dig som har, eller vill bygga, kompetens inom riskhantering, cybersäkerhet, regelefterlevnad, bolagsstyrning samt krisberedskap och integritet – ur ett teknik- och verktygsstödsperspektiv. Det finns mycket utrymme att växa professionellt och personligt i denna position!
   

  

 Som ServiceNow GRC konsult kommer du att;
   

  

* Spela en nyckelroll i att hjälpa våra kunder att stärka sin GRC-funktion (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) med hjälp av ServiceNow plattformen
* Vara involverad i olika kundengagemang inom olika branscher
* Vägleda och hjälpa kunder med råd om implementering och användning av ServiceNow inom primärt Governance Risk and Compliance
* Bidra till att utforma och anpassa lösningar som adresserar kundens behov inom risk- och säkerhetshantering, regelefterlevnad, leverantörsrisk, nödberedskap och integritet
* Samarbeta med multidisciplinära team för att säkerställa leveranser av hög kvalitet
