***Vi kombinerar branschkunskap med modern teknik för att bygga framtidens digitala verktyg för professionella kök.***

title: Azure & Power Platform Engineer
description:
Proven experience in Azure configuration and administration.
 



 Strong knowledge of Azure Active Directory, RBAC, and identity management. Hands-on experience with Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
 



 Familiarity with Azure security best practices and compliance standards.
 



 Proficiency in user administration, including onboarding, access provisioning, DLP management and license management.
 



 Configure and manage Azure services, including resource groups, virtual networks, storage accounts, and identity management.
 



 Implement and maintain Azure Active Directory (AAD) for user and group administration, including role-based access control (RBAC).
 



 Design, deploy, and manage Power Platform solutions (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI) integrated with Azure services. Monitor and optimize system performance, security, and compliance within Azure, On premise and Power Platform environments Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support application development and deployment. Maintain documentation for configurations, policies, and procedures.

title: Data & AI Platform - Center of Enablement Manager
description:
Are you excited by the idea of running a modern data platform that our engineers rely on every day? Join us in shaping our architecture, coaching teams, and turning best practice into golden paths that make great engineering the easy choice!
   

  

**Why Sandvik Coromant?**
 We’re a global leader where data, AI, and automation directly power real manufacturing impact – and where your platform becomes the backbone for product teams across the business. We don’t just lead the manufacturing industry – we’re reshaping it. As a global frontrunner in advanced manufacturing, we're on a journey to transform how we work and how we create value.
   

  

 The industry’s evolving fast, and we’re responding with smart, end-to-end solutions powered by bold ideas and passionate people. This is your chance to join a high-trust, empowered team that challenges the status quo and puts customers at the center of everything we do. Let’s shape the future of manufacturing together!
   

  

**Your mission**
 In this position, you lead the strategy, architecture, and operations of our Data and AI Experience Platform as well as our team of Platform Engineers and Architects. You ensure platform reliability, cost compliance, and vendor alignment while driving technical excellence and fostering a strong engineering culture. Acting as our go-to expert for critical challenges, you coach engineers, shape best practices, and enable autonomous product teams through optimized architecture and self-service capabilities. You define standards and services which make it easy for your team to do the right thing while experimenting and building autonomy, compliance, security, cost efficiency and reliability.
   

  

 You lead through example – continuously learning and experimenting with new technologies, building solutions hands-on, and coaching others. Create space and opportunities for team learning, foster collaboration, and confidently make informed technical decisions that inspire trust and growth. Adoption is your success currency, and you constantly find ways in which your team can improve Monthly Active Engineers and Monthly Active Champions on your platform.
   

  

**Location and flexibility**
 The location for this position is either Sandviken or Stockholm, with a hybrid setup where you can combine office and remote work.
   

  

**Your profile**
 We’re looking for someone with several years of experience in engineering and technology roles combined with some experience from leadership and people management. The ideal candidate has a background with at least one of the following roles: Data & Analytics Architect, Platform Architect or AI Architect. Acting in a global environment calls for excellent skills in English, verbally and in writing.
   

  

 You have proven expertise with:
   

  

* Cloud computing service providers, preferably Microsoft Azure.
* Lakehouse service providers, preferably Databricks.
* Embedded Data Warehouse and Analytics providers, preferably SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.
* Expert software engineering skills in Python, SQL, C# or similar languages.


 Success in this role comes from strategic thinking with hands‑on technical curiosity, bringing a natural drive to explore new ideas and solve complex challenges. You lead with openness and empathy, coaching others and building trust across teams while remaining calm and confident in your decisions. With a collaborative mindset and a genuine focus on customer needs, you create clarity, foster autonomy, and inspire a strong engineering culture grounded in learning, inclusivity, and continuous improvement.
   

  

**Our culture**
 Innovation is in our DNA and we’re proud to shape the future of manufacturing in close collaboration with our customers and partners. We believe in an inclusive, equal and open-minded culture, and we nurture our diversities to form a solid foundation for achieving great results. Add fair and rewarding benefits, as well as many different career opportunities in an inspiring global environment. Meet some of your future colleagues at our career page or visit our website, LinkedIn or Facebook page to get to know us further.
   

  

**Contact information**
 Are you curious to know more about this position? Please contact Anton Peters, Head of Data & AI, at anton.peters@sandvik.com
   

  

 We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contact in that matter.
   

  

 Union contacts (Sweden):
   

  

* Thomas Lilja, Unionen, +46 (0)70-261 04 82
* Tord Engström, Akademikerföreningen, +46 (0)76 106 62 87
* Peter Olsson-Andrée, Ledarna, +46 (0)70-222 48 55


 Recruitment Specialist: Therese Rutqvist
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We have an ongoing selection process in this recruitment and ask you to send your application as soon as possible and no later than February 27, 2026. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e-mail. Job ID: R0087443.
   

  

 As we aim for a fair recruitment process, we utilize assessment tools to safeguard objectivity. When you apply for this job, you will therefore receive an invitation via email to a personality and logic ability test. Feedback comes immediately after the test has been completed and the selection process begins after the application deadline.
   

  

 To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com

title: V.I.E. Sustainable Solutions
company: Societe Generale Corporate and Investment Banking - SGCIB
description:
Reference 260002NO
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 Development of the offering of sustainable investment, hedging, and financing solutions for Global Markets Activities.
   

  

 Responsibilities :
   

  

* Review and maintain the list of proprietary and external indices embedding ESG criteria; you will be expected to analyze their methodology and create marketing documentation. You may also contribute with the rest of the team to the construction of such proprietary indices embedding ESG criteria
* Monitor and contribute to develop the overall sustainable structured solutions offering: notably ensuring compliance with procedures, monitoring volumes and commercial activity, monitoring and helping to develop new partnerships
* Contribute to assisting the rest of the team in specific quantitative analysis requests, notably having access to various sources of ESG data and contents
* Participate in sustainable finance market studies, monitor the development of the sustainability sector, and monitor regulatory or political developments related to ESG issues
* Occasionaly, collaborate with the ESG Advisory team and participate in Societe Generale's cross-functional projects in the field of sustainable finance
* Prepare and participate in trainings and committees bringing together the ESG ambassadors within the sales, financial engineering and trading teams of the Global Markets Division
* Prepare the newsletters and share with the community any relevant communication related to ESG


**A questionnaire will be provided to you in order to check your VIE (Société Générale and Business France) eligibility. For an efficient analysis of your application, please make sure to truthfully answer all the questions.**
**Please note that it is mandatory to provide us a formal proof of your M2 diploma validation before the assignment start date. No VIE mission will start without this document. \*Grade sheets are not accepted as justification of diploma.**
**Required**
**Profile required**
**PLEASE NOTE that since this program is primarily an international development program, candidates cannot apply to a VIE assignment in their own country of citizenship.**
**To facilitate the examination of your application by our English-speaking managers, we thank you for applying in English.**
 Studies & experience:
   

  

* Graduate with a Master degree from Business/Engineering School or University with a specialization in Finance or Sustainable Finance
* A previous experience in Finance would be appreciated


 Language skills:
   

  

* Mandatory: Fluent in English


 Technical, operational & Soft skills:
   

  

* Good communication and presentation skills
* Management of complex projects
* Proficient command of MS Office, notably Excel
* Coding in Python is a plus


 The VIE assignment in a nutshell
   

  

 This VIE in Stockholm is to begin June 1st, 2026, but you need to plan 3 months between your application date and the beginning of your VIE assignment. It will last 12 months.
   

  

 The VIE is a specific contract, under Business France’s eligibility criteria, opened to candidates under 28 and from the member states of the European Economic Space. For further information (including your financial indemnities), please see Mon VIE-VIA Business France.
   

  

**Why join us**
 As soon as you arrive, you will be integrated into our teams and will learn every day alongside our experts, who will support you in your tasks. Gradually, you will become more independent in your projects, making this experience a real career accelerator. You will also discover all the diversity of our businesses, in a sector that is constantly evolving and innovating.
   

  

 At the end of your VIE, various opportunities could be offered to you, in France and abroad.
   

  

**Business insight**
 At Societe Generale, we are convinced that people are drivers of change, and that the world of tomorrow will be shaped by all their initiatives, from the smallest to the most ambitious.
   

  

 Whether you’re joining us for a period of months, years or your entire career, together we can have a positive impact on the future. Creating, daring, innovating and taking action are part of our DNA.
   

  

 If you too want to be directly involved, grow in a stimulating and caring environment, feel useful on a daily basis and develop or strengthen your expertise, you will feel right at home with us!
   

  

 Still hesitating?
   

  

 You should know that our employees can dedicate several days per year to solidarity actions during their working hours, including sponsoring people struggling with their orientation or professional integration, participating in the financial education of young apprentices, and sharing their skills with charities. There are many ways to get involved.

title: Head of Demand Gen (AI Startup)
description:
Most marketing hires at this stage want a playbook to follow. We need someone who writes the playbook. You will own a number, control how we market, and have an immense impact. That excites you, not makes you fearful.
 



  





 AutoRFP.ai automates how enterprise sales teams respond to RFPs, security questionnaires, and due diligence requests. Built model-agnostic from day one, deployed across 44 countries, growing 15% monthly, profitable, and bootstrapped. No VC committee approving your budget. No 18-month runway clock ticking in the background.
 



  





**Position Summary** 




 We're looking for a Head of Demand Generation to:
 


* Own the metric. ICP inbound demos booked is what you're measured on, and your success directly determines your upside
* Build the demand engine from strategy through execution, then scale the team around what works
* Operate at the intersection of enterprise marketing and AI, not just using AI tools, but betting on the channels that our legacy software competitors will be too slow to adopt



  





**Your Mission** 




 You'll inherit a team with strong conviction, a product with genuine traction, a few channels that work very, very well, and a budget to deploy.
 



  





 You'll work closely with the CEO, Co-Founder. You will have immense impact over all parts of marketing, from brand, to creative, to socials. It's your oyster. We have ideas. Some will be good, some won't be. It will be your call to say yes or no. You need the judgment to tell the difference, the conviction to push back, and the ability to execute your own strategy when you do.
 



  





 If you've spent the last few years owning a channel and being solely accountable for its outcomes, this is the step up. You'll own the entire function. The upside is uncapped, and so is the responsibility.
 



  





**In your first month you can expect to:** 



* Audit what's working today: where demos are coming from, what's converting, what's being left on the table
* Get deep into the product. Talk to customers and opportunities, you can't market what you don't understand, and our buyers are sophisticated



  





**In your first three months you can expect to:** 



* Have your core channels identified and producing pipeline
* Be running experiments across content, paid, events, or whatever you've identified as highest-leverage
* Have a clear point of view on where to double down and what to kill



  





**In your first six months you can expect to:** 



* Own a repeatable demand engine that's driving consistent inbound demo volume
* See inbound ICP demos improve, month on month. And have plans for how to keep up the growth
* Have a case for your first hire and a plan for how the function scales
* Be deeply embedded in the go-to-market motion alongside sales leadership



  





**What we're looking for:** 



* 3-6 years in B2B marketing with demonstrated ownership of at least one channel end-to-end. You can point to the numbers and say "I did that."
* Experience marketing to enterprise buyers, ideally in B2B SaaS (selling to sales/revenue leaders) or financial services (investor relations personas). You understand long sales cycles and multi-stakeholder buying committees.
* High creative taste. You care about how things look, how copy reads, and whether a campaign actually resonates with a senior buyer. Performance marketing metrics matter, but not at the expense of brand and quality.
* A bootstrapper's instinct for resource allocation. You'll have a real budget, but you think in terms of ROI per dollar, not "spend to plan."
* On the bleeding edge of AI and marketing. Not just using ChatGPT for copy. You have a thesis on which channels and formats are underpriced right now because incumbents haven't caught up.
* Hungry to be an IC who builds. You might want to lead a marketing team at a hyper-growth, profitable SaaS company one day. This is how you get there.



  





**The Culture** 




 We've built a team of people who are genuinely good at what they do. We give direct feedback, ask hard questions, and trust each other to deliver. When things break, we own it and fix it together. When we ship something great, we celebrate.
 



 This isn't a place to coast. But if you want to build something meaningful with people who care as much as you do, you'll love it here.
 



  





**Location & Office** 




 Stockholm: We have an office in Stockholm. We provide standing desks, brand new M3 Macs, 49" Ultra Wide monitors, and licenses to cutting-edge AI tools.
 



  





**Compensation** 




 834,117.82 SEK base + uncapped performance-based compensation tied directly to inbound demo generation. The better you perform, the more you earn, with no ceiling.
 


* Equipment includes latest MacBooks, 49" screens, and any software or AI tools that make you more effective. Monthly team lunches, annual off-sites, and future opportunities to visit our global offices.

title: AI Solutions Lead
description:
At Telenor Sweden, we are moving beyond AI experimentation to actual business value creation, integrating AI into the very fabric of our enterprise-wide processes. We are now looking for an
 **AI Solutions Lead** 
 to our Data Analytics & AI Team. In this role, you will hold the keys to unlocking true business value through the strategic use of AI. As AI Solutions Lead, you are the essential bridge between cutting-edge innovation and real-world application in one of the most dynamic sectors of the telecom industry.
   

  

**How You Will Make a Difference With Us**
 As an AI Solutions Lead, you will play a key role in driving the development, delivery, and scaling of AI solutions across Telenor Sweden. You lead AI and Automation experts to deliver high‑quality, end‑to‑end solutions aligned with our strategic priorities and seamlessly integrated into our IT landscape.
   

  

 You set and activate our enterprise‑wide AI strategy, defining the vision, priorities, governance, and roadmap in line with TnSE’s goals and the Nordic AI First program. You identify and scale high‑impact AI use cases from concept to production, ensure strong solution architecture, and drive innovation across teams. You also guide our RPA and Agents/Automations roadmap in line with evolving technologies and Google’s agentic framework.
   

  

 Through close collaboration with senior leaders and key stakeholders, you secure alignment and sponsorship to accelerate AI adoption. You uphold Responsible AI governance, ensuring ethical and compliant development, and guarantee that our AI solutions are robustly integrated for long‑term scalability and stability. Finally, you strengthen Nordic collaboration by promoting knowledge sharing and aligning AI practices across markets.
   

  

**Are you the one we’re looking for?**
 For this position, we are seeking a high‑energy, forward‑leaning AI specialist who is passionate about the transformative power of AI and GenAI. You are a self‑driven professional with a proven track record of turning cutting‑edge ideas into enterprise‑grade solutions on platforms like GCP and AWS. With an owner mindset, you thrive on taking concepts from initial PoC to scaled, hardened solutions. You excel in collaborative environments, bringing the strategic clarity needed to align complex technical architectures with tangible business value. You are equally comfortable engaging senior stakeholders and leading cross‑functional teams to build and scale next‑generation AI capabilities.
   

  

**Qualifications**
* Degree in Computer Science, Computer/Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field (e.g., Data Science, Physics).
* Proven experience leading AI/ML teams and fostering a high‑performance, value‑driven culture.
* Demonstrated success in building and scaling end‑to‑end AI and analytics solutions for real‑world applications across areas such as customer experience, operations, and commercial optimization.
* Strong technical background in AI, GenAI, and Agentic Automation.
* Experience managing complex AI/ML programs and driving cross‑functional collaboration across business, IT, and senior leadership.
* Excellent communication skills, able to translate complex technical concepts into clear insights for senior stakeholders.
* Extensive experience with cloud platforms (especially GCP), including BigQuery and Vertex AI, plus knowledge of frameworks and tools such as A2A, MCP, and Google’s Agent Development Kit.
* Proven ability to deliver business value through data‑driven innovation.
* Deep understanding of data architectures and secure data processing.
* Proficiency in English; telecom experience and Swedish proficiency are meriting.


**Our promise to you**
**We are better together.**
 Here, you’ll be part of a family-like and inclusive culture, built on strong shared values and leadership that empowers you with both trust and confidence. We believe in enabling each other to act and make decisions, while always having each other’s backs through ups and downs.
   

  

**We support relationships and societies.**
 Telenor plays a vital role in the society, giving your work a greater purpose. Every day, we create value for millions of people and maintain a customer-first approach where human relationships are at the heart of everything we do.
   

  

**We make room for life.**
 Work from home up to two days a week, if your role allows it. The rest of the time, we create magic together at the office. Leadership based on trust and flexible working hours helps you balance work and life. We support you through every stage of life with our benefit package, including collective agreements, occupational pensions, wellness allowances, and customized insurance solutions tailored to your needs.
   

  

**We invite you to a career in motion.**
 Your development is at the center of our culture. We dedicate at least 40 hours per year for you to deepen your expertise, explore new areas, and develop your role. Through Telenor Academy and other learning initiatives, you’ll have the tools and freedom to advance your career. If you want to broaden your horizons, we are present throughout the Nordics.
   

  

**Apply today!**
 Will you take the next step in your career with us? We look forward to your application! We review applicants on an ongoing basis, so don’t wait—reach out today. If you have any questions or want to know more, feel free to contact the hiring manager Asad Khan at
 **asad.khan@telenor.se** 
**.** 
 Please note that we cannot accept applications via email. The last day to apply is Sunday 15th of March 2026.
   

  

**Good to know**
 As part of Telenor’s policy, we conduct background checks on final candidates for all recruitments.

title: Data analyst
description:
**Vi söker en vass Data Analyst till Team Data & Analys, är du redo för en ny utmaning?** 



  


 På MedMera Bank tycker vi att det är viktigt att fatta beslut baserade på data och analys, därför är vårt team inom Data & Analys centralt för beslut på alla nivåer i banken. Gruppen har kommit långt i sitt arbete med maskininlärning och avancerad analys.
 


  


 Vi är ett litet företag med drygt 60 anställda som är snabbfotade i såväl beslut som implementation. Hos oss får du snabbt ta ansvar och lösa riktiga problem. Vi hjälper gärna våra kollegor att lyckas och våra medarbetarundersökningar visar att vi trivs och har roligt tillsammans. Teamet du kommer tillhöra kallas Data & Analys och består av tre data scientists, två data engineers och snart en data analyst.
 


  





  


**Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter** 



  


 Vi ansvarar för data, analys, rapportering och modellering av kreditrisk, prissättning och lönsamhet.
 **Ditt huvudfokus** 
 kommer ligga på att förstå vår data så att du kan
 **driva vårt analysarbete framåt** 
 . Du kommer också
 **ansvara för bankens validering av modeller** 
 , vilket ställer krav på både gedigna statistikkunskaper men också förmåga att dokumentera i rapportform. Valideringen rör flera områden såsom kreditrisk (IFRS 9) och Anti Financial Crime (AFC).
 


  


 Vi jobbar i Google Cloud Platform (bl.a. VertexAI och BigQuery) och programmerar i Python och SQL. Vår utvecklingsmiljö är modern och effektiv – vi tar hjälp av rätt verktyg på rätt plats och AI-stöd i arbetet är en självklarhet för att frigöra tid till det som verkligen gör skillnad.
 


  


 Hos oss på MedMera Bank kommer du att arbeta direkt mot bankens ledningsgrupp i ditt analysarbete. Detta innebär att du har stora möjligheter att påverka de beslut banken tar i viktiga frågor. Det ställer också krav på dig att inte bara räkna rätt utan också att kunna förklara på vad du kommit fram till på ett pedagogiskt sätt.
 


  


 Teamet du kommer arbeta i är välfungerande och utmärker sig genom att vi hjälps åt, har roligt och löser komplexa problem tillsammans. Vi är också bra på att styra upp sociala aktiviteter som AW, brädspelskväll eller liknande. Nya initiativ är varmt välkomna!
 


  





  


**Vem är du?** 



  


 Vi söker dig som står på en stabil grund inom matematik, maskininlärning och programmering. Utöver det har du också en vilja att lära dig det du behöver kunna för att ta ditt arbete hela vägen från analys / validering till presentation eller rapport. Du har en pragmatisk inställning till din kompetens och de tekniker du använder – det viktiga är hur de kan användas för att generera insikter och driva lönsamhet.
 


  


 Som person är du ödmjuk, gillar att samarbeta med andra, trivs med högt tempo och varierande arbetsuppgifter. I den här tjänsten finns det en stor möjlighet att lära sig både bank och dataanalys och du kommer få utmanande uppgifter från start. Vi tror att det är viktigt att du litar på din egen analytiska förmåga för att lyckas i tjänsten.
 


  

  

**Kvalifikationer**

 Vi söker dig som har;
 


  

* Universitetsexamen inom ett kvantitativt område, exempelvis matematik, statistik, data science eller motsvarande.
* Gedigna kunskaper i Python, eller annan programmeringserfarenhet som gör dig trygg oavsett miljö/språk.
* Grundläggande kunskaper i SQL.
* Egen drivkraft och förmåga att självständigt lära dig nya saker som du även kan applicera i arbetet för att lösa de problem du stöter på.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska språket då vi dokumenterar och rapporterar mestadels på svenska.


  


 Det är meriterande om du har;
 


  

* Kunskap om GCP (eller annan molntjänst).
* Kunskap om bank och produkter som privatlån.
* Kunskap om andra programmeringsspråk.
* Kunskap om rapporteringsverktyg såsom Looker, Streamlit eller annat
* Erfarenhet från liknande arbetsuppgifter.
* Ett dokumenterat intresse för programmering, exempelvis hobbyprojekt på Github.


  


 Vi fäster stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper. Vi strävar efter en inkluderande arbetsplats med en jämn könsfördelning och ser därför gärna kvinnliga sökande till just detta team.
 


  


 Välkommen med din ansökan så snart som möjligt då vi jobbar löpande med urval och intervjuer!

title: Data Engineer
description:
At Hive Streaming, we enable the world’s largest enterprises to use live video as a strategic tool for communication, alignment, and impact. Trusted by more than 130 global customers, Hive combines market-leading video distribution technology with unique communication analytics, helping organizations ensure flawless video experiences and measure how effectively messages reach, engage, and align employees.
   

  

 Our users are IT and Network professionals who rely on Hive every day to ensure flawless video delivery, Event Producers who ensure optimal video experience during internal company events, Leaders and Communications professionals who plan and execute powerful video-enabled strategies to deliver measurable impact.
   

  

 What do we do - really? No better way to explain what Hive actually does than with a video 🎥 Watch the video below to find out more 👇🏼
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for a versatile data engineer with strong software engineering skills to join our Partner Ecosystem team. In this role, you will be a major contributor in designing and implementing the big data pipelines - real-time and batch-powering our products. Moreover, you will contribute in enabling Hive Products by working closely with our partner video platforms, such as Microsoft Teams Town Hall and Vimeo!
   

  

**Working As a Data Engineer, You Will**
* Design and implement big data pipelines on top of our data platform.
* Develop solutions leveraging our tech stack: Spark/Scala, Databricks and Delta lake.
* Work on end-to-end delivery of data to our applications.
* Contribute to shaping our product by bringing insights and innovative ideas to discussions.
* Collaborate with the team to engineer high-quality and reliable solutions.
* Help analyze our data to improve our products


**Working In Partner Eco, You Will**
* Design and implement our clients for the collection of data
* Work closely with our partner developers to enable Hive with high quality
* Own the data from the collection to its manipulation
* Align and want to live by our core values: customer-centricity, innovation, and empowerment.


**More Details**
**Role type:** 
 Full-time with a standard probation period
   

  

**Start date:** 
 ASAP, depending on notice
   

  

**Working hours:** 
 Align with CET (Central European Time)
   

  

**Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden (Hybrid: ±2 days/week in the office), can consider Berlin, Germany
   

  

*🐶Just a heads up, we're a dog-friendly office!*
**Who We Are Looking For**
 We're looking for someone with a strong technical foundation, but just as importantly, the right attitude. At Hive, how you work is just as valuable as what you know. We also don’t expect you to tick every box. If you’re excited about this role and ready to learn, we’d love to hear from you!
   

  

**We Are Looking For Someone With**
* Bachelors within Computer Science or a similar field, or equivalent experience
* Expert in Apache Spark and/or other distributed data processing frameworks
* Experience in delivering tested and maintainable code in projects with multiple contributors
* Knowledgeable in cloud environments and familiar with common cloud-native data storages


**We See It As a Bonus If You Have**
* Experience in TypeScript or JavaScript
* Prior experience working with video streaming solutions, networking, and/or peer-to-peer
* Experience with mentorship or informal leadership


**And Just As Importantly, We Believe You Are**
* A team player, who shares information and expertise, promotes team effectiveness by facilitating and building on the ideas of others
* Resourceful, who are quick to change behavior to adapt to changing circumstances
* A problem solver, who loves challenges and finding creative, practical solutions
* A good communicator, seeking first to understand and then to be understood
* Service-minded, who has a visible approach and shows sincere interest in customers and their concerns


**Why join Hive?**
* Global Impact: Partner with some of the world’s largest enterprises, helping them achieve alignment and measurable success through live video.
* Dynamic Culture: Join a collaborative, inclusive, and diverse environment that values curiosity, rapid learning, and new perspectives.
* Empowered Leadership: Play a pivotal role in shaping Hive’s future while connecting directly with the customers who rely on our solutions.
* Pioneering Innovation: Be at the forefront of building strategic communication tools that empower enterprises to thrive amidst massive change.
* Great Benefits: Wellness stipend, pension, health insurance, extra vacation days, benefit bikes, and more!
* Flexibility: We value flexibility and trust. Whether you’re balancing personal commitments or exploring new ideas, you’ll find support and autonomy here with flexible working hours and a hybrid work model.


 Recruitment process: what to expect
   

  

 To apply, please submit your CV and answer a few short questions in lieu of a cover letter directly in the application form. Please submit all documents in English.
 **PLEASE NOTE:** 
 All applications will be reviewed after the winter holidays!
   

  

 Our hiring process is designed to be transparent and collaborative:
   

  

* Intro call: Meet our People & Talent Partner for a chat about your background, motivation, and what excites you about Hive. You’ll also learn more about our culture and how we work.
* Hiring manager interview: Meet Claudio, one of our Engineering Managers and your potential future manager. This conversation focuses on your experience, career goals, and how you could contribute to the team.
* At-home assessment: Complete a short at-home assessment on an example task to see how you'd engage in the type of work you'd be doing.
* Technical interview: The at-home assessment will form the basis of a technical discussion with members of the Partner Ecosystem team. Together, you’ll explore your approach to problem-solving and technical design.
* Data engineering interview: Meet a few members of our Data Guild for a final conversation focused on team fit, ways of working, and your approach to data engineering. This is also your chance to ask questions and get a deeper sense of our data culture at Hive.
* Reference checks: We take security seriously and conduct two reference checks as part of our compliance with internal and regulatory requirements.
* Decision: Timely feedback and next steps - our goal is to complete the process in an efficient and timely manner, depending on your availability.

title: Sales Operations Manager, EMEA Sales Operations
description:
**Description**
 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the leading cloud provider, offering virtualized infrastructure, storage, networking, messaging, analytics, and other web computing services to customers all over the world. AWS operates a globally distributed environment at massive levels of scale, offering over 200 fully featured services to millions of active customers around the world—including the fastest-growing startups, largest enterprises, and leading government agencies—to power their infrastructure. Within the AWS WW Commercial Sales (WWCS) organization, the Sales Strategy and Operations team is responsible for supporting Leadership, Sales, and Operational teams in achieving organizational objectives and driving continuous improvements in field productivity. We own and deliver “run the business” (RTB) operational cadences, build new mechanisms to support organizational growth and scale, and drive organizational initiatives.
   

  

 AWS is looking for a Sales Operations Manager to join their Global Sales Strategy & Operations team in Europe North. This role combines hands-on analytics with operational excellence, where you'll use data analysis and modeling to support Sales Leaders and their teams. You'll play a key role in maintaining business health and driving data-informed decisions across the region. This role combines technical expertise with business acumen to analyze complex datasets from multiple sources. You'll be instrumental in delivering monthly reports and conducting specialized business analyses that directly impact our decision-making processes.
   

  

**What You'll Bring**
* Strong technical skills in data extraction and modeling
* Exceptional organizational abilities
* Self-motivation and adaptability to work in an ambiguous, fast-paced environment
* Experience handling large datasets
* Ability to support multiple sales teams simultaneously


**Success In This Role Means**
* Collaborating effectively with sales teams, operations teams and internal users
* Developing and implementing new reporting solutions
* Creating accurate, impactful reports
* Communicating complex data insights to both technical and non-technical audiences
* Supporting Europe North Sales teams with territory analysis and operational activities


 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Data Analysis & Insights –
* Conduct comprehensive analysis of diverse datasets to uncover meaningful business insights
* Design and deliver both regular and on-demand business analytics to meet stakeholder requirements
* Strategic Planning & Reporting
* Maintain monthly health of the business models
* Create and manage various financial modeling projects
* Extract and analyze data using Excel and other data management platforms
* Metrics & Performance Management
* Monitor and evaluate existing performance metrics
* Identify opportunities for new metrics development
* Transform complex data into actionable recommendations
* Business Intelligence
* Deliver strategic insights to guide business decisions
* Provide data-driven recommendations through detailed analysis
* Create customized reports to address specific business needs


**Basic Qualifications**
* Experience with sales CRM tools such as Salesforce or similar software
* Experience using Microsoft Excel to manipulate and analyze data
* Experience defining, refining and implementing sales processes, procedures and policies or equivalent
* Experience with AWS/Cloud Computing


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience working within a high-growth, technology company
* Experience in finance, business management and sales operations
* AWS Certified Cloud practitioner/AI practitioner


 Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
   

  

 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 - AWS EMEA SARL (Sweden Branch) - G54
   

  

 Job ID: A3175691

title: Site Reliability Engineer
description:
**About the Company** 




  





 At FDJ UNITED, we don't just follow the game, we reinvent it. FDJ UNITED is one of Europe’s leading betting and gaming operators, with a vast portfolio of iconic brands and a reputation for technological excellence. With more than 5,000 employees and a presence in around fifteen regulated markets, the Group offers a diversified, responsible range of games, both under exclusive rights and open to competition. We set new standards, proving that entertainment and safety can go hand in hand. Here, you’ll work alongside a team of passionate individuals dedicated to delivering the best and safest entertaining experiences for our customers every day. We’re looking for bold people who are eager to succeed and ready to level-up the game. If you thrive on innovation, embrace challenges, and want to make a real impact at all levels, FDJ UNITED is your playing field. Join us in shaping the future of gaming. Are you ready to LEVEL-UP THE GAME?
 



  





**Site Reliability Engineer** 




 Are you an experienced, proactive engineer who enjoys identifying opportunities for improvement and bringing new ideas to the table? Do you keep up to date with industry best practices and take pride in turning insights into practical solutions? We're looking for a Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) to join our Platform Delivery and Reliability Services (PDRS) team and help us evolve our Observability Platform with fresh perspectives and steady innovation.
 



 At FDJ UNITED, we believe in an automation-first approach to designing infrastructure services that empower our brands worldwide. Here, your voice matters. We want someone who takes initiative, proposes improvements, and actively shapes our tools and processes for greater resilience and scalability.
 



 By joining us, you'll become part of a forward-thinking team where your expertise drives innovation and your ideas make a real impact. We work agile, with a strong focus on quality, collaboration, and personal growth. If you're ready to elevate your career and help shape the future of our platform services, this is the role for you.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities:** 



* Architect, build, and maintain large-scale, distributed telemetry pipelines and observability platforms that provide real-time insight into system
* Ensure that you adhere to the Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) obligations for your role.
* Identify and raise any non-compliance incidents promptly to your line manager.
* Challenge processes, policies and projects that will negatively impact compliance within the Group.
* Complete all mandatory compliance training assigned to you.
* Reach out to the Compliance Teams if unsure of any of your compliance obligations or the requirements are unclear.
* performance and reliability.
* Design, and integrate cutting-edge solutions with an automation-driven mindset.
* Act as a subject matter expert, collaborating with development teams to optimise instrumentation, observability tooling, and reliability strategies.
* Proactively prevent and troubleshoot production issues to keep our systems stable and performant.
* Boost reliability across products and services by implementing best practices and innovative tools.
* Work closely with Development and Infrastructure teams to seamlessly weave observability requirements—metrics, traces, logs, alerts—into new systems' designs.



  





**Skills and Experience:** 



* Proven experience as a Site Reliability Engineer with hands-on skills in logging platforms such as Splunk, ElasticSearch, Graylog, FluentD, or Vector.
* Strong coding skills in scripting languages like Bash, Python, or Golang, including code review experience.
* Deep expertise in Linux OS and distributed systems.
* Solid background with Time Series Databases and visualization (especially Grafana).
* Experience with alerting and monitoring tools like Prometheus, Icinga, Datadog, or Nagios.
* Familiarity with CD/CI tools (Jenkins, GitLab, GitHub Actions) and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible, CloudFormation).
* Knowledge of container orchestration platforms (Kubernetes, Nomad, OpenShift) and cloud environments (AWS, Azure, OpenStack) is a plus.
* Interest or experience in AI and AI tools is considered an advantage.



  





***Hybrid Work Disclaimer:***




*This role is based on a* 
***hybrid working model***
*. We require employees to be onsite in the office at least* 
***two days per week***
*, with flexibility to work remotely on the remaining days.* 




  





 Our Way Of Working
 



 Our world is hybrid.
 



 A career is not a sprint. It’s a marathon. One of the perks of joining us is that we value you as a person first. Our hybrid world allows you to focus on your goals and responsibilities and lets you self-organise to improve your deliveries and get the work done in your own way.
 



 Application Process
 



 We believe talent knows no boundaries. Our hiring process focuses solely on your skills, experience, and potential to contribute to our team. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and evaluate each candidate based on merit, regardless of personal characteristics as the age, gender, origin, religion, sexual orientation, neurodiversity or disability.

title: Senior Manager till Onsite Solutions
description:
*Är du en erfaren ledare inom ekonomiområdet med en passion för att driva förändring och utveckling? KPMG Onsite Solutions söker nu en Senior Manager till vårt team i Stockholm, där du får möjlighet att leda och coacha vårt team av engagerade konsulter inom ekonomi. Har du arbetat som ekonomikonsult, controller, redovisare eller i liknande roller och vill ta nästa steg i karriären? Drivs du av att utveckla och inspirera andra kollegor? Då kan det vara dig vi söker! Hos oss får du chansen att arbeta med både ledarskap och ekonomi, samtidigt som du bidrar till vår affärsutveckling och lönsamhet.*
**Om oss**
 KPMG Onsite Solutions är en social och sammansvetsad avdelning med team i Stockholm, Göteborg och Malmö. Vi erbjuder interimslösningar inom Finance, BI och Data Analytics och efterfrågan på våra tjänster är hög. Bland våra kunder finns några av Sveriges största bolag, små- och mellanstora bolag och spännande start-ups. Som Senior Manager hos oss får du en unik möjlighet att bidra med din expertis och realisera dina visioner.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Senior Manager på Onsite Solutions kommer du att ha ett övergripande ansvar för vårt team i Stockholm. Du leder och coachar konsulter och teamleaders och ingår i ledningsgruppen för att bidra till avdelningens utveckling och lönsamhet. Ditt ansvar omfattar bland annat att leda personal, driva förändringsprojekt och att delta i ledningsgruppen.
   

  

 ,
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* Minst 8 års erfarenhet av kvalificerat ekonomiarbete såsom controlling, redovisning, CFO-roll eller motsvarande
* Tidigare erfarenhet från konsultverksamhet såsom konsultbyrå, management consulting eller motsvarande
* Akademisk examen inom ekonomi
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att leda team och/eller konsulter
* Förmåga att bidra till affärsutveckling och försäljning
* God förståelse för ekonomisk styrning, processförbättring och förändringsledning
* Stark kommunikativ förmåga på svenska och engelska
* Vana att arbeta med Excel och affärssystem på avancerad nivå


**Vi ser det som meriterande om du har:**
* Erfarenhet av roller som interimskonsult
* Vana att arbeta nära ledningsgrupper och styrelser
* Internationell erfarenhet eller samarbete med globala team


**Vad vi erbjuder**
 Som Senior Manager hos oss får du möjlighet att bli kollega med några av Sveriges vassaste specialister inom ekonomi. Du kommer att arbeta i en dynamisk och stimulerande arbetsmiljö i hjärtat av Stockholm, där du blir en del av ett tight team med en sammanhållning utöver det vanliga. Hos oss har du chansen att påverka hur Onsite Solutions växer och utvecklas. Vi erbjuder ett fritt arbete med stort eget ansvar, där du får möjlighet att arbeta med några av Sveriges mest intressanta bolag.
   

  

**Vill du vara med och göra skillnad med oss?**
 Skicka in din ansökan idag och bli en del av en inspirerande arbetsplats med engagerade kollegor. Har du frågor eller funderingar gällande tjänsten eller rekryteringsprocessen är du varmt välkommen att kontakta Sofie Pemer, rekryterare, på sofie.pemer@kpmg.se.
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!

title: Lösningsarkitekt .NET, Stockholm
description:
**Jobbeskrivning**
**Vi utvecklar system som får samhällsviktiga funktioner att fungera. Nu söker vi dig som vill vara med och bygga dem.**
 Som lösningsarkitekt inom .NET har du ett övergripande ansvar för den tekniska lösningen i våra agila teamleveranser. Du omsätter kundens behov till hållbara arkitekturer och är delaktig från tidig dialog till färdig lösning, ofta baserat på Microsofts teknikstack. Uppdragen finns inom bland annat vård, energi och telekom och varierar i komplexitet.
   

  

 Tekniskt arbetar du med
 **.NET, C#, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework** 
 samt
 **JavaScript** 
 och
 **TypeScript** 
 med ramverk som
 **React** 
 . Du är även involverad i
 **Azure** 
 och
 **AWS, CI CD, API** 
 design och mikrotjänstarkitektur. Rollen kombinerar arkitektansvar med hands on utveckling och teknisk rådgivning. Läs mer här.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som**
* Har minst 10 års erfarenhet av systemutveckling inom .NET, och har arbetat i rollen som lösningsarkitekt i minst ett par år
* Har arbetat fullstack med moderna webbramverk i JavaScript eller TypeScript, gärna React eller Angular
* Har förmåga att ta ett helhetsgrepp om lösningar, från arkitektur till kod och implementation
* Är trygg i kunddialoger och har vana att leda tekniska workshops
* Har erfarenhet av molnlösningar, gärna Azure
* Trivs i konsultrollen och har god förståelse för affären
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på både svenska och engelska
* Ser värdet i kunskapsdelning och agerar gärna mentor för andra i teamet


**Att jobba på Sopra Steria**
 Hos oss får du ett stabilt bolag med stora kunder och resurser, i kombination med entreprenörsanda och stark teamkänsla. Vi erbjuder utmanande och varierande uppdrag där du kan bidra tekniskt för att göra skillnad i samhället. Med kollektivavtal, marknadsmässiga löner, flexibilitet och en kultur präglad av kunskapsdelning och samarbete vill vi ge dig en riktigt bra arbetsmiljö för att lyckas. Läs mer om hur det är att jobba hos oss här.
   

  

**Rekryteringsprocessen**
 I rollen ställs höga krav på både kompetens och säkerhetsmedvetenhet. Inför en eventuell anställning genomför vi bakgrundskontroll, och vid intervju behöver du kunna uppvisa giltig legitimation. Processen består av tre intervjuer, personlighet och logiktest, referenstagning samt bakgrundskontroll. Denna kombination ger oss en bred och rättvis bild av dig, och ger dig möjlighet att visa din kompetens och potential från flera perspektiv.
   

  

 📩 Har du frågor om rollen? Kontakta gärna Hanna Korell på hanna.korell@soprasteria.com.

title: Enterprise Architect
description:
**We help the world run better**
 At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed.
   

  

**What You’ll Build**
 As part of SAP’s Enterprise Architecture team, you will lead customers through their digital transformation journeys — from strategy to execution. You’ll help shape their NorthStar Enterprise Architecture, guide clean core adoption, and translate architectural visions into tangible outcomes through SAP solutions, including the Business Technology Platform (BTP).
   

  

**You Will**
* Advise customers on future-proofing their IT landscapes, helping them simplify, modernize, and accelerate innovation.
* Collaboratively develop a capability/application as-is and target the enterprise architecture model based on SAP’s reference industry architecture
* Drive the Clean Core imperative by reducing complexity, consolidating systems, and streamlining processes.
* Enable organizations to realize the full value of SAP BTP through integration, extensibility, and process optimization.
* Advise and drive AI capability Adoption across the customer organization
* Act as a trusted advisor across business and IT functions, helping customers align technology decisions with strategic business goals.
* Work collaboratively with senior stakeholders to drive adoption and consumption in order to deliver exceptional Cloud Subscription Renewals


**What You Bring**
 You are a seasoned professional who combines deep Enterprise Architecture expertise with business acumen and a genuine empathy for customers.
   

  

**Must-have**
* 10+ years of experience in customer-facing roles (e.g., Enterprise Architect, Consultant, Program Manager, Solution Architect, Customer Advisory,…)
* Track record in helping customers/stakeholders drive business adoption of technology solutions in order to unlock tangible business value and maximise consumption
* Proven track record in delivering enterprise business transformations through large-scale application delivery and implementation programs
* Demonstrated SAP S/4HANA application/functional knowledge and advising and applying Clean Core framework
* Exceptional communication, influencing, and negotiation skills in English, with the ability to engage senior stakeholders across IT and business.
* Ability to operate in large and complex organizations with multiple stakeholders


**Nice To Have**
* Experience using SAP LeanIX and Signavio to document architectures and business processes.
* Familiarity with SAP Cloud solutions / Business Capabilities and SAP Business AI
* Familiarity with BTP Integration Suite / BTP Build
* Professional Certifications such as SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework (LeanIX EAM), SAP Certified Professional–Enterprise Architect (P\_SAPEA\_2023), or TOGAF.


**Where you belong**
 Join a diverse and passionate team of
 **SAP Enterprise Architects** 
 driving meaningful digital transformation across industries.
   

  

 We collaborate closely with various SAP teams and SAP partners, turning strategy into execution with a focus on innovation and business impact.
   

  

**Locations:**
 Stockholm, Gothenburg, Helsinki, Oslo or Copenhagen (Kindly only apply for one location)
   

  

**Bring out your best**
 SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.
   

  

**We win with inclusion**
 SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.
   

  

 SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.
   

  

 For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.
   

  

 Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements.
   

  

 Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor.
   

  

**AI Usage in the Recruitment Process**
 For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process.
   

  

 Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.

title: People Tech Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 As our People Tech Manager, you’ll own the design, implementation, and scaling of our HR systems, tools, and data infrastructure that power our people operations as we grow 3× over the next 10 months.
   

  

 Reporting to and working closely with the People Tech Lead, you’ll work hands-on across our HR tech stack while partnering closely with People Enablement, IT, Finance, Legal, and business leaders.
   

  

**Your mission:** 
 ensure our people infrastructure scales ahead of the company, not behind it.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
**System Management & Optimisation**
* Own, manage and improve our people technology stack, including HRIS, ATS, document management systems, and the cloud-based integrations that connect them.
* Configure systems for scalability, security, and regulatory compliance, proactively anticipating future requirements.
* Maintain data accuracy, system reliability, and technical documentation to ensure a single source of truth for people data.


**Automation & Integration**
* Design and build integrations that create seamless, reliable data flows between systems.
* Identify manual, repetitive processes across the employee lifecycle and automate them.
* Build custom solutions where off-the-shelf tools fall short.


**Process Design & Enablement**
* Translate complex HR operations and regulatory requirements into scalable, system-driven solutions that embed compliance by design while increasing operational velocity and efficiency.
* Enable the People Enablement team and managers to use systems confidently and effectively, designing intuitive, user-centric processes that require little to no training—even for non-technical users.
* Design and document scalable processes across the full employee lifecycle, from hire to retire.


**Who You Are**
 You are a systems-oriented builder. When you encounter manual processes, you instinctively think about how they could be automated, standardised, or eliminated.
   

  

 You balance technical execution with long-term thinking. You are comfortable configuring complex HRIS workflows and integrations, preferably code-first solutions (Typescript) while planning for future requirements at a significantly larger scale (1000+ employees and beyond).
   

  

 You approach internal tools with a product mindset. You consider usability, reliability, and adoption across the entire organisation, and you understand when to optimise for robustness versus when to deliver a pragmatic, iterative solution.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
* 2-4+ years of experience managing internal technology in high-growth environments.
* Deep, hands-on experience with modern HRIS platforms and the broader HR technology ecosystem.
* Ideally, a strong Typescript foundation to maintain, operate, and evolve cloud-based integrations and event-driven workflows.
* Strong project management skills, with experience delivering multi-system implementations in fast-paced environments.
* Experience with people analytics, data governance, and dashboarding/reporting.
* Comfort making decisions with incomplete information and iterating quickly.


**Why This Role Matters**
 People technology is foundational to how Legora scales. Every system you implement, process you automate, and data structure you design directly shapes organisational speed, data integrity, and regulatory compliance —enabling impact at pace.
   

  

 If you want to build world-class people operations through intelligent systems, automation, and thoughtful technical design—and see the impact of your work immediately and at scale—this role is for you.
   

  

**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Erfaren Test Architect/Manager sökes till spännande projekt inom Testautomation
description:
**Company Description**
 SEGULA Technologies är en ledande ingenjörskoncern med global närvaro som stärker konkurrenskraften inom alla större industrisektorer: fordonsindustrin, flygindustrin, energi, järnväg, sjöfart, läkemedelsindustrin och petrokemi. Koncernen är verksam i 30 länder med 140 kontor världen över och vårdar starka relationer med sina kunder genom expertisen hos sina 15 000 anställda. Innovation är kärnan i SEGULAs strategi, med storskaliga projekt som sträcker sig från tidiga studier till industrialisering och produktion.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Är du en driven Test Architect eller Test Manager som brinner för teststrategi och automation? Då kan detta vara din nästa utmaning!
   

  

 Vi på SEGULA Technologies söker nu en erfaren Test Architect/Manager för ett uppdrag inom testautomation för vår kund i Stockholm.
   

  

 I rollen som Test Architect/Manager kommer du att ha en nyckelposition i att utforma och implementera teststrategier och ramverk för automatiserade mjukvarutester. Det här arbetet innebär att komma in på avdelningen, se över befintlig miljö (HW/SW) samt ta fram en plan för komplett automatiserad teststrategi. Vi tror att detta arbete tar omkring 3-6 månader, men det finns även möjlighet till förlängning, beroende på resultat.
   

  

**Ditt Arbete Kommer Bland Annat Att Inkludera**
* Ansvar för strategin och arkitekturförslaget av framtida testautomationslösningar.
* Ta fram ett tydligt och komplett förslag på teststrategi som presenteras för ledningen.
* Säkerställa att testmetodiken som föreslås håller högsta standard.
* Ta fram kostnadskalkyl och eventuella investeringsbehov.


**Qualifications**
**Vem är du?**
 Vi söker dig med lång erfarenhet som Test Architect eller Test Manager. Du har en djup teknisk kompetens och ett stort fokus på kvalitetssäkring.
   

  

**Kvalifikationer Vi Söker**
* Gedigen erfarenhet av Teststrategier och Testautomation.
* Förmåga att ta ägandeskap över tekniska lösningar och sprida kunskap.
* Stark kunskap inom C#/.NET.
* Erfarenhet av test i en distribuerad mjukvaru-/systemmiljö, gärna med HIL/SIL/MIL.
* ISTQB Advanced certifiering eller motsvarande kunskap är meriterande.


**Additional Information**
 Observera att urval och intervjuer sker löpande, och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum. Skicka därför in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.
   

  

 Vi strävar efter en rekryteringsprocess fri från diskriminering och fördomar. Vi välkomnar sökande oavsett kön, könsidentitet eller uttryck, ålder, etnisk tillhörighet, religion eller annan trosuppfattning, funktionsvariation, sexuell läggning eller bakgrund.

title: Lead, Energy Market Development
description:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following:
 **Helsinki, Finland; Copenhagen, Denmark; Stockholm, Sweden** 
 .
 **Minimum qualifications:**
* Bachelor's degree in Technology, Science, Law, Policy, Economics, or equivalent practical experience.
* 6 years of experience negotiating and structuring agreements or leading business development.
* Experience working on policy or regulation in Europe related to electricity markets or electricity networks.
* Ability to communicate in Finnish and English fluently in order to communicate with local stakeholders in Finland and other markets.


**Preferred qualifications:**
* Experience with power transmission and distribution, telecommunications infrastructure, data center technology, industrial-class electrical and cooling technology, or other related technical areas.
* Experience in European energy markets, specifically regarding grid tariffs, market design, and regulatory frameworks.
* Experience working within an energy regulator or the regulatory unit of an energy supplier, Transmission System Operator (TSO), Distribution System Operator (DSO), or large electricity user.
* Comprehensive knowledge of Finland's energy market design, structure, and stakeholder ecosystem.
* Ability to grasp complex technical product aspects and develop data-driven recommendations and action plans.
* Ability to integrate public policy with business strategy and corporate development.


**About The Job**
 Google's infrastructure needs go far beyond server computers. As Google's products and services scale the globe, the Strategic Negotiation team works behind the scenes to secure infrastructure for Google's future -- everything from underwater cables to physical data center space. As a Strategic Negotiator, you combine your deep market knowledge of a given sector with tech industry savvy to negotiate cost-effective solutions to support Google's infrastructure growth. You'll work with specific project teams on negotiating deals, managing vendor and partner relationships and presenting deal recommendations to our Tech leadership. Your successful negotiations have the potential to save Google millions of dollars in operating costs and impact every part of the business.
   

  

 You will be part of the team responsible for Google’s global energy portfolio where you will work at the forefront of some of the most challenging energy and infrastructure policies and regulatory issues of the day.
   

  

 As the Lead for our Energy Market Development team, you will work on energy-related policy, regulations, and advocacy efforts throughout Europe. You will lead the assessment of new proposals regarding their impact on our data center energy supply and carbon-free energy programs, provide data-driven recommendations, and ensure the execution of projects towards our policy objectives.
   

  

 This is an external-facing role where you will interact with multiple stakeholders at the local, national, and European level.
   

  

 Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Lead strategic evaluations of regulatory and policy environments in Europe at both EU and national (with strong focus on the Nordic region) levels, regarding electricity industry trends, stakeholder interests, and electricity institutional structures. Assess risks and opportunities for Google’s data center electricity supply portfolio.
* Lead the development of strategic energy positions for Google’s electricity supply needs in established and new markets, design of regulatory and market engagement strategies, and implementation of tactics. Work cross-functionally on Google’s global infrastructure and public policy to develop policy and market positions and communicate to key constituencies.
* Lead advocacy work through energy trade associations, industry partners, non-profit groups, and other partners to advance Google’s strategic energy priorities. Draft internal energy positions and respond to external public consultation on energy.
* Support energy supply negotiations, working with site and energy strategic negotiators.


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Data Engagement Partner – Data, Analytics & AI
description:
**Role Description**
 We are looking for an experienced Data Engagement Partner in Data, Analytics & AI – working with a strong BFSI domain understanding
   

  

**Client Relationship & Revenue Protection**
* Establish and maintain strong relationships with client stakeholders across CDO, LOB Data Leaders and data teams
* Act as a trusted onsite advisor – anticipate risks to existing programs, manage escalations early and protect renewals/expansions


**Opportunity Sensing & Pipeline Creation**
* Proactively identify opportunities by onsite presence: attending relevant forums, “walking the floors,” listening for pain points and translating them into structured opportunities

**Consultative Solutioning (Data + AI + Experience)**

* Willingness to travel in the Nordics


**Skills**
**Mandatory Skills:** 
 Data, Analytics, AI, BFSI Domain, Data Architecture, Data Modernization, Data Governance & Management

title: Technical Account Manager
description:
As a Technical Account Manager you will play a critical role in enhancing client experiences and enabling global growth.
   

  

 You will thrive in this position if you are collaborative, strategic, have a global mindset, and bring a passion for finance and technology to a fast-paced, high-impact environment.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Provide overall technical support services to clients in accordance with the Support and Maintenance Agreement.
* Collaborate closely with product engineering and project management teams, acting as a bridge between customers and internal teams to proactively manage expectations.
* Own and coordinate client incidents, ensuring they are monitored, regularly updated, and resolved within agreed response and resolution times.
* Take accountability for client SLAs and deliver internal and external KPI reporting.
* Contribute to the Client Success strategy to drive growth and enhance support satisfaction, in a hands-on role requiring strong technical understanding of Nasdaq products and technology.


**Required Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or a related field, or equivalent experience in engineering, product management, quality assurance, or similar roles.
* Strong client orientation with excellent communication, interpersonal, and relationship-building skills.
* Solution-oriented and customer-focused, with an understanding of engineering and quality assurance.
* Solid technical background, including enterprise software implementation experience and good Unix/Linux and cloud knowledge, with the ability to learn new technologies quickly.
* Good understanding of capital markets and financial systems.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience in a technology-driven or fintech environment.
* Data-driven communication skills and the ability to present to diverse audiences.


 This position is located in Stockholm, Sweden, and offers a hybrid work environment with at least 3 days per week in the office.
   

  

**Benefits & Rewards**
 We offer a competitive, well-rounded rewards package that supports you and your family - inside and outside work.
   

  

 Actual pay depends on your skills, experience, education, and location. In addition to base pay, we offer short-term incentives (bonus) and long-term incentives (equity), where applicable, as well as the following benefits:
   

  

**What We Offer**
* Competitive base salary
* Annual bonus
* Annual equity grant
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan offering discounted company shares
* Pension plan
* 32 paid vacation days
* 6 additional days off per year
* Extra vacation time based on tenure
* Work from (almost) anywhere – up to 20 days/year
* Paid time off to volunteer
* Health insurance
* Sports club membership reimbursement
* 24/7 mental health support for you and your family
* Global mentoring program
* Unlimited access to e-learning platforms
* Hybrid work setup
* Modern and comfortable work environment with fresh fruit, snacks, and weekly fika breaks


**Come as You Are**
 Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, color, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law.
   

  

 We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request an accommodation.

title: People Insights Platform Lead
description:
**Sandvik Group**
 Sandvik is a global, high-tech engineering group providing solutions that enhance productivity, profitability and sustainability for the manufacturing, mining and infrastructure industries. We are at the forefront of digitalization and focus on optimizing our customers’ processes. Our world-leading offering includes equipment, tools, services and digital solutions for machining, mining, rock excavation and rock processing. In 2024 the Group had approximately 41,000 employees and revenues of about SEK 123 billion in more than 150 countries.
   

  

 Are you excited about shaping the future of datadriven HR? At Sandvik, we’re on a journey to build a worldclass People Insights capability — and we’re looking for a
 **People Insights Platform Lead** 
 to help us get there. This is a unique opportunity for someone who enjoys being both
 **handson** 
 and
 **strategic** 
 . You build the core of our Groupwide People Insights Platform while steering its longterm direction. If you love creating scalable analytics products, enabling selfservice insights, and driving real impact across a global organization, this role is for you.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As our People Insights Platform Lead, you take full ownership of the People Insights Platform, shaping the semantic layer, data structures, and shared metrics that ensure one unified source of truth across Sandvik. You build and maintain the core components of the platform, including Power BI models and reusable templates.
   

  

 In this role, you also lead delivery endtoend together with the data engineers. You’ll run intake, backlog refinement, and sprint cycles, while remaining handson by developing dashboards, prototypes, data models, and other semantic assets yourself. Ensuring that every release is stable, scalable, and of high technical quality will be a central part of your responsibility.
   

  

 Collaboration is key. You work closely with Business Area People Analytics Partners, Group PA, and IT, translating technical decisions into clear, businessfriendly explanations. You’ll help drive adoption across the organization by hosting walkthroughs, onboarding sessions, and practical training.
   

  

 You oversee and actively contribute to data engineering work by partnering with data engineers, engaging in data ingestion and transformation processes, and validating and preparing datasets to enable reliable analytics and reporting pipelines. Finally, you help advance our longterm analytics vision. You’ll lay the groundwork for AIdriven insights, crossdomain analyses, and advanced analytics, while promoting continuous improvement and innovation across the platform.
   

  

**What You Bring**
 You bring solid experience in building enterprisegrade Power BI solutions, supported by strong skills in DAX, data modelling, and designing semantic layers. You’re handson with ETL/ELT, SQL, or similar data transformation tools, and you know how to collaborate effectively with stakeholders, translate business needs into analytics solutions, and deliver highquality products. Experience with HR data is a plus.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 Curious, collaborative, and driven — your way of working helps elevate our global People Analytics capability. Your leadership style combines clarity with openness, and you enjoy empowering others while building great solutions yourself.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 At Sandvik, you’ll join a truly global environment built on innovation, teamwork and continuous improvement. We offer:
   

  

* Professional and personal development opportunities
* A culture rooted in our values: Winning Together, Curiosity, Responsibility and Customer Focus
* Supportive colleagues and a workplace where diversity and inclusion matter


 Learn more about us on our Stories Hub, LinkedIn or Facebook.
   

  

**Apply today**
 We look forward to receiving your application no later than
 **28 February 2026** 
 .
   

  

 Apply via
 **home.sandvik/career** 
 using reference
 **R0088261** 
 .
   

  

**Questions?**
 Contact us at
 **HRBenelux@sandvik.com** 
 .
   

  

 (Please note: We have predetermined our advertising channels and kindly decline outreach from agencies.)
